questions.stem: Which of the following transactions is within the scope of ASC 718 (Compensation — Stock Compensation)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A company issues shares to acquire a business in a business combination

choices.label: B
choices.text: A company grants stock options to employees as part of their compensation package

choices.label: C
choices.text: A company issues shares in an initial public offering

choices.label: D
choices.text: A company repurchases its own shares on the open market

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-15 applies to all share-based payment transactions in which an entity acquires goods or services by issuing (or offering to issue) its shares, stock options, or other equity instruments. Employee stock option grants are the most common example. Business combinations (A) are covered by ASC 805, IPOs (C) are equity transactions, and share repurchases (D) are treasury stock transactions under ASC 505.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-15は、企業が株式・ストックオプション等の持分商品を発行して財・サービスを取得する全ての株式報酬取引に適用される。従業員へのストックオプション付与が典型例。企業結合(A)はASC 805、IPO(C)は資本取引、自社株買い(D)はASC 505の範囲。

questions.subtopic: Fair Value Measurement at Grant Date
questions.stem: Under ASC 718, when should the fair value of a stock option granted to an employee be measured?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the grant date

choices.label: B
choices.text: At the vesting date

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the exercise date

choices.label: D
choices.text: At each reporting date until exercise

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-30-2 requires that the fair value of equity-classified stock options be measured at the grant date — the date at which the employer and employee reach a mutual understanding of the key terms and conditions of the award. This grant-date fair value is then used to recognize compensation expense over the requisite service period. The fair value is not subsequently remeasured for equity-classified awards.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-2により、持分分類のストックオプションの公正価値は付与日（grant date）に測定する。付与日とは雇用者と従業員が報酬の主要条件について合意した日である。この公正価値を必要勤務期間にわたり報酬費用として認識する。持分分類の場合、その後の再測定は行わない。

questions.stem: A company grants 10,000 stock options to an employee on January 1, Year 1. The options have a grant-date fair value of $8 per option and vest over a 4-year service period (cliff vesting). What is the compensation expense recognized in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Total compensation cost = 10,000 options × $8 = $80,000. Under ASC 718-10-25-1, compensation cost is recognized on a straight-line basis over the requisite service period for cliff-vesting awards. Year 1 expense = $80,000 / 4 years = $20,000. The expense is recognized regardless of whether the options are ultimately exercised.
questions.explanationJa: 報酬総額 = 10,000個 × $8 = $80,000。崖式権利確定(cliff vesting)の場合、ASC 718-10-25-1に基づき必要勤務期間にわたり定額法で費用認識。Year 1費用 = $80,000 ÷ 4年 = $20,000。

questions.stem: Which of the following is a market condition under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The employee must remain employed for three years

choices.label: B
choices.text: The company must achieve earnings per share of $5.00

choices.label: C
choices.text: The company's stock price must reach $50 per share

choices.label: D
choices.text: The employee must complete a specific project

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-20 defines a market condition as a condition upon which the exercise price, exercisability, or other pertinent factors depend that is related to the achievement of a specified stock price or a specified amount of intrinsic value. A target stock price of $50 (C) is a market condition. Continued employment (A) is a service condition. EPS target (B) is a performance condition. Project completion (D) is also a performance condition.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-20では、市場条件を株価や本質的価値の達成に関連する条件と定義する。目標株価$50(C)が市場条件。継続雇用(A)は勤務条件、EPS目標(B)は業績条件、プロジェクト完了(D)も業績条件。

questions.stem: A company grants stock options with an exercise price of $30 when the stock price is $30. The fair value of each option using an option-pricing model is $12. Under ASC 718, what amount per option is used to measure compensation cost?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $12 (fair value)

choices.label: B
choices.text: $0 (intrinsic value)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $30 (stock price)

choices.label: D
choices.text: $42 (stock price plus fair value)

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-30-2 requires the use of fair value to measure compensation cost for stock options, not intrinsic value. Even when options are granted at-the-money (exercise price equals stock price, making intrinsic value $0), the fair value from an option-pricing model captures the time value and expected volatility. The $12 fair value per option is used to determine total compensation expense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-2は、ストックオプションの報酬費用測定に公正価値を使用することを要求する。アットザマネー（行使価格=株価、本源的価値$0）であっても、オプション価格モデルによる公正価値$12が時間的価値とボラティリティを反映する。

questions.stem: A company initially estimates that 10% of stock options will be forfeited. In Year 2, the company revises this estimate to 15%. How should the change in estimated forfeitures be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Prospectively, by adjusting expense in future periods only

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a cumulative catch-up adjustment in the period of the change

choices.label: C
choices.text: By restating prior period financial statements

choices.label: D
choices.text: As an extraordinary item in the income statement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-35-3, changes in estimated forfeitures are accounted for as a change in estimate, which results in a cumulative catch-up adjustment in the period the estimate is revised. The cumulative compensation cost recognized to date is adjusted to reflect the revised forfeiture estimate, with the difference recognized in the current period. Prior periods are not restated.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-3により、失効見積りの変更は見積りの変更として処理し、変更期間に累積的キャッチアップ調整を行う。当期までの累積報酬費用を修正後の失効率で再計算し、差額を当期に認識。過年度の修正再表示は行わない。

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT an input to the Black-Scholes option-pricing model used to estimate the fair value of stock options under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expected term of the option

choices.label: B
choices.text: Risk-free interest rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expected forfeiture rate

choices.label: D
choices.text: Expected volatility of the underlying stock

questions.explanation: The Black-Scholes model inputs include: (1) exercise price, (2) current stock price, (3) expected term, (4) expected volatility, (5) risk-free interest rate, and (6) expected dividends. The expected forfeiture rate is NOT an input to the option-pricing model itself. Forfeitures are accounted for separately by adjusting the number of awards expected to vest, not by adjusting the per-option fair value.
questions.explanationJa: Black-Scholesモデルの入力変数は、行使価格、株価、予想期間、予想ボラティリティ、リスクフリー金利、予想配当の6つ。予想失効率はモデルの入力変数ではなく、権利確定見込数の調整として別途反映する。

questions.stem: A company grants 9,000 stock options on January 1, Year 1 with a fair value of $10 per option. The options vest in three equal annual installments (3,000 each year). Using the straight-line method for each tranche, what is the total compensation expense in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under graded vesting, each tranche is treated as a separate award and recognized using the straight-line method over its own vesting period. Tranche 1 (3,000 options, 1-year vest): $30,000 / 1 = $30,000 in Year 1. Tranche 2 (3,000 options, 2-year vest): $30,000 / 2 = $15,000 in Year 1. Tranche 3 (3,000 options, 3-year vest): $30,000 / 3 = $10,000 in Year 1. Total Year 1 = $30,000 + $15,000 + $10,000 = $55,000. Note: The straight-line method applied to the entire award would give only $90,000 / 3 = $30,000 per year, which understates Year 1 expense under graded vesting.
questions.explanationJa: グレーデッド・ベスティング（段階的権利確定）では、各トランチを独立した付与として扱い、それぞれの権利確定期間でストレートライン法を適用する。第1トランチ(3,000個、1年): $30,000 ÷ 1年 = $30,000。第2トランチ(3,000個、2年): $30,000 ÷ 2年 = $15,000。第3トランチ(3,000個、3年): $30,000 ÷ 3年 = $10,000。Year 1合計 = $30,000 + $15,000 + $10,000 = $55,000。なお、全体一括のストレートライン法では$90,000 ÷ 3 = $30,000/年となり、グレーデッド・ベスティングの正しい計算とは異なる。

questions.stem: A company grants 5,000 stock options contingent on achieving cumulative revenue of $10 million over 3 years. At the end of Year 1, management assesses the performance condition as probable of being met. The grant-date fair value is $6 per option. What is the cumulative compensation expense recognized through Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-20, compensation cost for awards with performance conditions is recognized only when the achievement of the condition is probable. Since management assesses the condition as probable at Year 1 end, cumulative expense through Year 1 = (5,000 × $6) × (1/3) = $10,000. If the condition were not probable, no expense would be recognized until it becomes probable, at which point a cumulative catch-up would be required.
questions.explanationJa: 業績条件付き報酬は、条件達成が「probable（蓋然性が高い）」と判断された場合にのみ費用認識する。Year 1末に達成見込みと判断したため、累積費用 = (5,000 × $6) × 1/3 = $10,000。達成見込みなしと判断されれば費用ゼロ。

questions.stem: A company grants stock options that vest only if the stock price reaches $75 within 4 years. The grant-date fair value, which incorporates the market condition using a Monte Carlo simulation, is $5 per option. After 3 years, it becomes clear the stock price target will not be met. What is the effect on compensation expense?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All previously recognized expense is reversed

choices.label: B
choices.text: Compensation expense continues to be recognized over the remaining service period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expense recognition stops immediately, but previously recognized expense is not reversed

choices.label: D
choices.text: An impairment loss is recorded

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-20, a market condition is reflected in the grant-date fair value estimate (using a lattice or Monte Carlo model), and compensation cost is recognized over the requisite service period regardless of whether the market condition is met. Unlike performance conditions, the outcome of a market condition does not affect whether compensation cost is recognized — only the service condition matters. Expense is never reversed for market condition failure.
questions.explanationJa: 市場条件は付与日公正価値の算定に反映される（モンテカルロ等）。ASC 718-10-25-20により、市場条件の達成・未達にかかわらず、必要勤務期間にわたり報酬費用を認識し続ける。業績条件と異なり、市場条件未達でも費用の取消しは行わない。

questions.stem: A company grants cash-settled stock appreciation rights (SARs) to employees. How should the compensation expense be measured at each reporting date?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the grant-date fair value, with no subsequent remeasurement

choices.label: B
choices.text: At intrinsic value only at the settlement date

choices.label: C
choices.text: At fair value at each reporting date until settlement, with changes recognized in income

choices.label: D
choices.text: At the lower of grant-date fair value or current fair value

questions.explanation: Cash-settled SARs are classified as liabilities under ASC 718-30-25. Liability-classified awards are remeasured at fair value at each reporting date until settlement, with changes in fair value recognized as compensation expense (or credit) in the income statement. This contrasts with equity-classified awards, which are measured once at the grant date and not subsequently remeasured.
questions.explanationJa: 現金決済型SARはASC 718-30-25に基づき負債に分類される。負債分類の報酬は決済まで各報告日に公正価値で再測定し、変動額を損益（報酬費用）として認識する。持分分類の報酬（付与日に一度だけ測定）とは対照的。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Asset from Stock Compensation
questions.stem: A company recognizes $200,000 of cumulative stock compensation expense for book purposes by Year 2. The tax deduction will occur at exercise. Assuming a 25% tax rate and the award is equity-classified, what deferred tax asset is recognized?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-740-25, a deferred tax asset (DTA) is recognized based on the book compensation expense. DTA = cumulative book expense × tax rate = $200,000 × 25% = $50,000. The tax deduction does not occur until the options are exercised (for NQSOs) or shares are sold (for ISOs with a disqualifying disposition). The temporary difference creates a DTA that will reverse upon exercise.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-740-25により、帳簿上の報酬費用に基づき繰延税金資産(DTA)を認識する。DTA = 累積帳簿費用 × 税率 = $200,000 × 25% = $50,000。税務上の控除はオプション行使時（NQSO）まで発生しないため、一時差異として繰延税金資産を計上。

questions.stem: A company modifies stock options by reducing the exercise price from $40 to $30. Immediately before the modification, the fair value of the original options is $8 per option. Immediately after, the fair value of the modified options is $14 per option. What is the incremental compensation cost per option?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-20-35-3, when equity-classified awards are modified, the incremental compensation cost is measured as the excess of the fair value of the modified award over the fair value of the original award immediately before the modification. Incremental cost = $14 - $8 = $6 per option. The original grant-date fair value continues to be recognized, plus the incremental $6 is recognized over the remaining service period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-3により、持分分類の報酬を変更した場合の追加報酬費用は、変更直前の原報酬の公正価値と変更後の公正価値の差額で測定する。追加費用 = $14 - $8 = $6/個。原報酬の付与日公正価値の認識は継続し、追加$6を残存勤務期間で認識。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, a company grants 20,000 stock options with a fair value of $15 per option. The options cliff-vest after 3 years of service. Estimated forfeitures are 5%. What is the compensation expense for Year 2?
questions.explanation: Total compensation cost = 20,000 options × $15 FV × (1 − 5% forfeiture rate) = $285,000. Straight-line over 3-year cliff vest: annual expense = $285,000 / 3 = $95,000. Trap (A) $85,000 uses a 15% forfeiture rate ($255,000 / 3). Trap (C) $100,000 ignores forfeitures ($300,000 / 3). Trap (D) $90,000 uses a 10% forfeiture rate ($270,000 / 3).

questions.stem: Which of the following stock-based payment arrangements should be classified as a liability rather than equity under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Stock options that the employee can only exercise by purchasing shares at the exercise price

choices.label: B
choices.text: Restricted stock units that settle in shares upon vesting

choices.label: C
choices.text: Stock appreciation rights that will be settled in cash at the employee's election

choices.label: D
choices.text: Restricted stock with a service condition only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-15, awards that will or may be settled in cash are classified as liabilities. Cash-settled SARs (where the employee receives cash equal to the stock price appreciation) are classified as liabilities because the entity has an obligation to transfer cash. Equity-settled instruments — standard stock options (A), share-settled RSUs (B), and service-condition restricted stock (D) — are classified as equity.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-15により、現金で決済される（またはされうる）報酬は負債に分類する。現金決済型SAR(C)は株価上昇分を現金で支払う義務があるため負債。通常のストックオプション(A)、株式決済RSU(B)、勤務条件のみの制限付株式(D)は持分に分類。

questions.stem: A company grants performance-based stock options. During Year 2, the company modifies the performance target, making achievement more likely. Before modification, achievement was deemed improbable (no expense recognized). After modification, achievement is probable. The original award fair value was $100,000 and the modified fair value is $120,000. What is the cumulative catch-up expense at the modification date (end of Year 2 of a 3-year service period)?
questions.explanation: When a modification changes a performance condition from improbable to probable, the entity must recognize a cumulative catch-up. The total cost is the greater of: (1) original grant-date fair value ($100,000) or (2) modified fair value ($120,000). Since the modified value is higher, total cost = $120,000. Cumulative expense through Year 2 (2/3 of service period) = $120,000 × 2/3 = $80,000. Since no prior expense was recognized ($0), the full $80,000 is caught up at the modification date.
questions.explanationJa: 業績条件が「達成見込みなし」から「達成見込みあり」に変更された場合、累積キャッチアップが必要。報酬総額は原報酬$100,000と変更後$120,000の大きい方 = $120,000。Year 2末までの累積費用 = $120,000 × 2/3 = $80,000。従前認識額$0のため、$80,000全額をキャッチアップ。

questions.stem: A company recognized $300,000 of cumulative compensation expense for NQSOs. At exercise, the actual tax deduction is $400,000. The tax rate is 25%. What is the excess tax benefit recognized, and where is it reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $25,000, recognized as a reduction to income tax expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: $100,000, recognized in additional paid-in capital

choices.label: C
choices.text: $25,000, recognized in additional paid-in capital

choices.label: D
choices.text: $100,000, recognized as a reduction to income tax expense

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-740-35 (post ASU 2016-09), all excess tax benefits (and deficiencies) are recognized in the income statement as a component of income tax expense. DTA based on book expense = $300,000 × 25% = $75,000. Actual tax benefit = $400,000 × 25% = $100,000. Excess tax benefit = $100,000 - $75,000 = $25,000, recognized as a reduction to income tax expense. Prior to ASU 2016-09, excess benefits went to APIC.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09以降、超過税務便益は全額損益計算書の法人税等で認識する。帳簿ベースDTA = $300,000 × 25% = $75,000。実際税務便益 = $400,000 × 25% = $100,000。超過分 = $100,000 - $75,000 = $25,000を法人税等の減少として認識。ASU 2016-09以前はAPICに計上していた。

questions.stem: A company grants equity-settled SARs with a grant-date fair value of $50,000 that vest over 2 years. At the end of Year 1, the fair value of the SARs is $60,000. What journal entry does the company record at Year 1-end?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Compensation Expense $30,000, Credit SAR Liability $30,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Compensation Expense $25,000, Credit APIC-SARs $25,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Compensation Expense $30,000, Credit APIC-SARs $30,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Compensation Expense $25,000, Credit SAR Liability $25,000

questions.explanation: Equity-settled SARs are classified as equity instruments under ASC 718. Like equity-classified stock options, they are measured at grant-date fair value and not remeasured. Year 1 expense = $50,000 / 2 years = $25,000. The credit goes to APIC (equity), not to a liability. The current fair value of $60,000 is irrelevant for equity-classified awards — only the grant-date fair value of $50,000 is used.
questions.explanationJa: 持分決済型SARは持分に分類され、付与日公正価値で測定し再測定しない。Year 1費用 = $50,000 ÷ 2年 = $25,000。貸方はAPIC（持分）であり負債ではない。現在の公正価値$60,000は持分分類では無関係。

questions.stem: A company has 100,000 stock options outstanding with an exercise price of $20. The average market price during the period is $50, and net income is $500,000. Weighted-average shares outstanding are 1,000,000. Using the treasury stock method, how many incremental shares are added to the diluted EPS denominator?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 100,000 shares

choices.label: B
choices.text: 40,000 shares

choices.label: C
choices.text: 60,000 shares

choices.label: D
choices.text: 0 shares (anti-dilutive)

questions.explanation: Under the treasury stock method (ASC 260-10-45-22): (1) Assume all 100,000 options are exercised, generating proceeds of 100,000 × $20 = $2,000,000. (2) The proceeds would repurchase $2,000,000 / $50 = 40,000 shares at the average market price. (3) Incremental shares = 100,000 issued - 40,000 repurchased = 60,000. Since the exercise price ($20) is less than the market price ($50), the options are dilutive and the 60,000 incremental shares increase the diluted EPS denominator.

questions.stem: Under ASC 718 (as amended by ASU 2018-07), how should share-based payments to nonemployees (such as consultants) be measured?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the fair value of the goods or services received, measured at each reporting date

choices.label: B
choices.text: At the fair value of the equity instruments issued, measured at each reporting date until the performance completion date

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the fair value of the equity instruments issued, using the same measurement principles as employee awards

choices.label: D
choices.text: At the fair value of the equity instruments issued, measured at the grant date, with the performance completion date as the measurement date

questions.explanation: ASU 2018-07 simplified nonemployee share-based payment accounting by aligning it with the guidance for employees under ASC 718. Nonemployee awards are now measured at the grant-date fair value of the equity instruments, using the same fair value measurement principles as employee awards. The previous requirement to remeasure at each reporting date until the performance completion date was eliminated.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2018-07により、非従業員への株式報酬の会計処理が従業員と統一された。非従業員への報酬も付与日の持分商品の公正価値で測定し、従業員報酬と同じ測定原則を使用する。従前の各報告日における再測定（業績完了日まで）の要求は廃止された。

questions.stem: Entity A grants 10,000 restricted stock units (RSUs) to an employee on January 1, Year 1, when the stock price is $50. The RSUs vest on December 31, Year 3 (cliff vesting). No dividends are paid. What is the total compensation cost?

choices.label: C
choices.text: The fair value at the vesting date

choices.label: D
choices.text: $500,000 adjusted for the probability of vesting

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-30-2, equity-classified awards (including RSUs settled in shares) are measured at grant-date fair value, which is not subsequently remeasured. Total compensation = 10,000 units × $50 = $500,000, recognized ratably over the 3-year vesting period ($166,667 per year). The stock price on the vesting date is irrelevant for equity-classified RSUs.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-2により、資本分類のRSUは付与日公正価値で測定し再測定しない。総報酬 = 10,000 × $50 = $500,000。3年間で均等認識（年$166,667）。権利確定日の株価は関係ない。

questions.subtopic: Stock Option - Intrinsic Value Method Election
questions.stem: Under ASC 718, when may a nonpublic entity elect the intrinsic value method instead of the fair value method for stock options?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A nonpublic entity may elect to use intrinsic value for all equity-classified awards

choices.label: B
choices.text: A nonpublic entity may elect to use intrinsic value only for liability-classified awards

choices.label: C
choices.text: The intrinsic value method is not available under ASC 718

choices.label: D
choices.text: Both public and nonpublic entities may choose either method

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-30-4, a nonpublic entity may make an accounting policy election to measure equity-classified awards at intrinsic value (stock price minus exercise price at each reporting date) instead of fair value. This election is available because nonpublic entities may have difficulty estimating fair value of options using models like Black-Scholes. Public entities must use fair value.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-4により、非公開企業は資本分類の報酬について、公正価値法の代わりに本源的価値法（各報告日の株価−行使価格）を選択できる。非公開企業はBlack-Scholes等のモデルによる公正価値見積りが困難なため。公開企業は公正価値法が必須。

questions.stem: Entity B grants 20,000 options with a 3-year service condition and a performance condition (achieving $50M revenue). At the end of Year 1, achievement is considered improbable. At the end of Year 2, it becomes probable. The grant-date fair value is $10 per option. What is the cumulative compensation expense at the end of Year 2?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-20, compensation cost for an award with a performance condition is recognized only when achievement is probable. In Year 1, no expense is recognized (improbable). In Year 2, when the condition becomes probable, cumulative catch-up is required. Total cost = 20,000 × $10 = $200,000. Cumulative through Year 2 = $200,000 × 2/3 = $133,333. Year 2 expense = $133,333 (catch-up from $0).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-20により、業績条件付報酬は達成が見込まれる場合にのみ費用認識。Year 1は不確実で$0。Year 2で確実となり累積キャッチアップ。累計 = $200,000 × 2/3 = $133,333。Year 2費用 = $133,333。

questions.stem: Entity C grants options with a market condition requiring the stock price to reach $80. The grant-date fair value (incorporating the market condition using a Monte Carlo simulation) is $6 per option. After the vesting period, the stock price never reaches $80. How is compensation expense affected?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Compensation expense is reversed because the condition was not met

choices.label: B
choices.text: Compensation expense is NOT reversed; it is fully recognized over the service period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only 50% of the compensation expense is recognized

choices.label: D
choices.text: No expense was recognized because the condition was never probable

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-30-14, the effect of a market condition is reflected in the grant-date fair value estimate (via pricing models like Monte Carlo). Once the grant-date fair value is determined, compensation cost is recognized over the requisite service period regardless of whether the market condition is subsequently achieved. This is a key difference from performance conditions, where expense is only recognized when achievement is probable.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-14により、市場条件の影響は付与日公正価値に反映される（モンテカルロシミュレーション等）。報酬費用は市場条件の達成の有無にかかわらず、勤務期間にわたり認識する。業績条件との重要な違い。

questions.stem: Entity D grants 5,000 phantom stock units (cash-settled) with a 2-year vesting period. Grant-date fair value is $30 per unit. At the end of Year 1, fair value is $35. At the end of Year 2 (vesting date), fair value is $40. What is the compensation expense in Year 2?
questions.explanation: Cash-settled awards are liability-classified under ASC 718-30-35-3 and remeasured at fair value each reporting period. Year 1: Cumulative expense = 5,000 × $35 × 1/2 = $87,500. Year 2: Cumulative expense = 5,000 × $40 × 2/2 = $200,000. Year 2 expense = $200,000 − $87,500 = $112,500. The liability is settled at $200,000 upon vesting.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-30-35-3により、現金決済報酬は負債分類で各期に公正価値で再測定。Year 1累計 = 5,000 × $35 × 1/2 = $87,500。Year 2累計 = 5,000 × $40 = $200,000。Year 2費用 = $112,500。

questions.stem: Entity E has an employee stock purchase plan (ESPP) with a 15% discount and a 6-month look-back feature. Under ASC 718, is this plan compensatory?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No, because the discount is less than 20%

choices.label: B
choices.text: Yes, because it has both a discount exceeding 5% and a look-back feature

choices.label: C
choices.text: No, because it meets all Section 423 requirements

choices.label: D
choices.text: Yes, but only because of the look-back feature

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-50-25-1, an ESPP is compensatory if any of the following exists: (1) discount exceeds 5%, (2) the plan includes a look-back feature, or (3) participation is not available to substantially all employees. This plan has BOTH a 15% discount (>5%) AND a look-back feature, making it clearly compensatory. Meeting IRC Section 423 requirements does not automatically make a plan noncompensatory under GAAP.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-50-25-1により、ESPPは(1)割引率5%超、(2)ルックバック条項あり、(3)実質的に全従業員が参加できない場合に報酬型。本プランは15%割引とルックバック条項の両方があり明確に報酬型。IRC§423の要件充足はGAAP上の非報酬型を意味しない。

questions.stem: Entity F modifies unvested stock options by extending the vesting period from 3 years to 5 years without changing other terms. The fair value immediately before modification is $12 per option and immediately after is $12 per option. How is the modification accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No change in total compensation; remaining unrecognized cost is spread over the new 5-year period

choices.label: B
choices.text: Additional compensation of $0 is recognized; remaining cost is accelerated

choices.label: C
choices.text: Remaining unrecognized compensation is recognized over the extended vesting period from the modification date

choices.label: D
choices.text: The original compensation is reversed and new cost is recognized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-20-35-3, when a modification does not change the fair value (incremental value = $0), the entity recognizes at minimum the original grant-date fair value. The remaining unrecognized compensation cost is recognized over the new remaining requisite service period (from the modification date through the end of the new 5-year vesting period). Total compensation does not change, but the recognition pattern shifts.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-3により、修正で公正価値が変わらない場合（増分$0）、元の付与日公正価値を最低限認識する。未認識残高を修正日から新権利確定期間にわたり認識。総報酬は変わらないが認識パターンが変わる。

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT an input to the Black-Scholes option pricing model used under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expected term of the option

choices.label: B
choices.text: Expected volatility of the stock

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expected earnings per share growth rate

choices.label: D
choices.text: Risk-free interest rate

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-55-27, the inputs to the Black-Scholes model include: (1) stock price at grant date, (2) exercise price, (3) expected term, (4) expected volatility, (5) risk-free interest rate, and (6) expected dividend yield. Expected EPS growth rate is NOT an input. The model values the option based on stock price dynamics, not earnings projections.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-55-27により、Black-Scholesモデルのインプットは：(1)付与日株価、(2)行使価格、(3)予想期間、(4)予想ボラティリティ、(5)リスクフリー金利、(6)予想配当利回り。予想EPS成長率はインプットではない。

questions.stem: Entity G grants 9,000 options that vest in three equal annual tranches (3,000 per year). The grant-date fair value per tranche is: Tranche 1 (1-year) = $5, Tranche 2 (2-year) = $7, Tranche 3 (3-year) = $8. Using the graded vesting method, what is Year 1 compensation expense?
questions.explanation: Under the graded vesting method (ASC 718), each tranche is treated as a separate award with its own service period. All three tranches begin accruing expense at the grant date. Year 1 expense: Tranche 1 (1-year service period) = 3,000 × $5 × 1/1 = $15,000. Tranche 2 (2-year service period) = 3,000 × $7 × 1/2 = $10,500. Tranche 3 (3-year service period) = 3,000 × $8 × 1/3 = $8,000. Total Year 1 expense = $15,000 + $10,500 + $8,000 = $33,500. The straight-line alternative over the 3-year total period would yield $20,000/year ($60,000 ÷ 3), but that is not the graded vesting method.
questions.explanationJa: 段階的権利確定法（ASC 718）では各トランシェを別個の報酬として扱い、付与日から全トランシェの費用認識を開始する。Year 1費用：トランシェ1（1年確定）= 3,000 × $5 × 1/1 = $15,000。トランシェ2（2年確定）= 3,000 × $7 × 1/2 = $10,500。トランシェ3（3年確定）= 3,000 × $8 × 1/3 = $8,000。合計 = $15,000 + $10,500 + $8,000 = $33,500。なお定額法では $60,000 ÷ 3年 = $20,000/年となるが、これは段階的権利確定法ではない。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-09, what policy elections are available for accounting for forfeitures of stock compensation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Estimate forfeitures at grant date only, with no subsequent revision

choices.label: B
choices.text: Either estimate expected forfeitures or recognize forfeitures as they occur (actual method)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Always recognize forfeitures as they occur; estimation is no longer permitted

choices.label: D
choices.text: Estimate forfeitures using a prescribed actuarial table

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-09 (ASC 718-10-35-2A), entities have an accounting policy election to either: (1) estimate expected forfeitures at grant date and revise as needed (the traditional approach), or (2) recognize forfeitures as they occur (accounting for actual forfeitures). This election applies to all share-based payment awards and must be consistently applied.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09（ASC 718-10-35-2A）により、(1)付与日に見積失効率を見積もり必要に応じて修正する方法、または(2)実際の失効発生時に認識する方法のいずれかを会計方針として選択できる。全ての株式報酬に一貫適用が必要。

questions.stem: How should a nonpublic entity estimate expected volatility when using the fair value method for stock options under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Use the historical volatility of a public entity in the same industry as a peer company

choices.label: B
choices.text: Use zero volatility since the stock is not publicly traded

choices.label: C
choices.text: Use an average industry index volatility calculated by the SEC

choices.label: D
choices.text: Nonpublic entities are prohibited from using the fair value method

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-55-37, when a nonpublic entity cannot reasonably estimate the expected volatility of its own stock price, it should consider the historical, expected, or implied volatilities of similar public entities. The entity selects peer companies with similar size, leverage, and industry and uses their volatility as a substitute for its own. Using zero is not appropriate, and the fair value method is available (though not required) for nonpublic entities.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-55-37により、非公開企業が自社株のボラティリティを合理的に見積れない場合、類似する公開企業のボラティリティを参照する。規模、レバレッジ、業種が類似する企業を選定。ゼロは不適切。

questions.subtopic: Award with Service and Performance Conditions
questions.stem: Entity H grants 15,000 options that vest after 3 years of service AND achievement of a revenue target. At grant date, fair value is $8 per option. At the end of Year 1, achievement is probable. At the end of Year 2, achievement becomes improbable. What is the cumulative expense at the end of Year 2?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-20, when a performance condition is not probable of being met, no compensation cost is recognized. At the end of Year 1, the condition was probable, so expense of 15,000 × $8 × 1/3 = $40,000 was recognized. At the end of Year 2, the condition becomes improbable, requiring a reversal of all previously recognized compensation. Cumulative expense = $0. If the condition later becomes probable again, a cumulative catch-up will be recorded.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-20により、業績条件の達成が見込まれない場合、報酬費用は認識しない。Year 2末に不確実となったため、Year 1で認識した$40,000を戻し入れ、累計費用 = $0。後に再び確実となれば累積キャッチアップ。

questions.stem: Entity J has fully vested stock options for 1,000 shares with an exercise price of $25. The APIC - Stock Options balance for these options is $8,000. When the options are exercised (par value $1 per share), what is the journal entry?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Cash $25,000 and APIC - Stock Options $8,000; credit Common Stock $1,000 and APIC $32,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Cash $25,000; credit Common Stock $25,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Cash $25,000 and APIC - Stock Options $8,000; credit Common Stock $33,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Cash $25,000; credit Common Stock $1,000 and APIC $24,000

questions.explanation: Upon exercise: Cash = 1,000 × $25 = $25,000 (debit). APIC - Stock Options is reclassified: $8,000 (debit to remove). Common Stock at par = 1,000 × $1 = $1,000 (credit). APIC = $25,000 + $8,000 − $1,000 = $32,000 (credit). The previously recognized compensation cost (in APIC - Stock Options) is transferred to APIC upon exercise.
questions.explanationJa: 行使時：現金$25,000借方。APIC-ストックオプション$8,000を借方で振替。普通株式$1,000（額面）貸方。APIC = $25,000 + $8,000 − $1,000 = $32,000貸方。以前認識した報酬費用がAPICに振り替えられる。

questions.stem: Entity K reprices its employees' underwater options from an exercise price of $50 to $30 (current stock price is $30). The fair value before modification is $3 per option, and after modification is $10 per option. There are 10,000 options with $20,000 of unrecognized compensation remaining. What incremental cost is added?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-20-35-3, the incremental compensation cost from a modification is the excess of the fair value of the modified award over the fair value of the original award immediately before modification. Incremental cost = ($10 − $3) × 10,000 = $70,000. Total remaining compensation = unrecognized original cost ($20,000) + incremental cost ($70,000) = $90,000, recognized over the remaining vesting period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-3により、修正による増分報酬 = 修正後公正価値と修正直前の公正価値の差額。増分 = ($10 − $3) × 10,000 = $70,000。残存報酬合計 = $20,000 + $70,000 = $90,000。

questions.stem: All else being equal, how does an increase in the expected dividend yield affect the fair value of a stock option calculated using the Black-Scholes model?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increases the fair value

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decreases the fair value

choices.label: C
choices.text: No effect on the fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increases fair value for calls but decreases for puts

questions.explanation: Under the Black-Scholes model, an increase in expected dividend yield decreases the fair value of a call option. This is because dividends reduce the expected stock price appreciation during the option term. Option holders do not receive dividends (unless specified), so higher expected dividends make the option less valuable. This is reflected in the dividend yield input to the model, which reduces the calculated fair value.
questions.explanationJa: Black-Scholesモデルでは、予想配当利回りの増加はコールオプションの公正価値を低下させる。配当はオプション期間中の株価上昇期待を減少させる。オプション保有者は配当を受け取らないため、配当が高いほどオプション価値は低下する。

questions.stem: Entity L grants 2,000 shares of restricted stock to an employee on January 1, Year 1 with immediate vesting. The grant-date fair value is $40 per share. The employee elects Section 83(b) for tax purposes. What is the compensation expense recognized in Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $80,000 recognized immediately on the grant date

choices.label: B
choices.text: $0 because the shares are restricted

choices.label: C
choices.text: $80,000 recognized over the expected holding period

choices.label: D
choices.text: $40,000 recognized in Year 1 and $40,000 in Year 2

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-35-2, compensation cost is recognized over the requisite service period, which is the period during which an employee is required to provide service in exchange for the award. Since the restricted stock vests immediately, the entire requisite service period is the grant date. Total compensation = 2,000 × $40 = $80,000, recognized immediately. The Section 83(b) election is a tax matter and does not affect the GAAP accounting.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-2により、報酬費用は必要勤務期間にわたり認識。即時権利確定のため勤務期間は付与日のみ。総報酬 = 2,000 × $40 = $80,000を即時認識。Section 83(b)選択は税務上の問題でGAAP処理に影響しない。

questions.subtopic: SAR - Equity vs Cash Settlement Comparison
questions.stem: Entity M grants stock appreciation rights (SARs) that can be settled in either cash or shares at the entity's discretion. Under ASC 718, how should these SARs be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As equity because the entity controls the settlement method

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a liability because cash settlement is possible

choices.label: C
choices.text: As equity if equity settlement is more likely

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a liability until settlement method is determined

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-14, when the entity has the choice between cash and equity settlement, the award is classified as equity because the entity can avoid the obligation to transfer cash by choosing to settle in shares. The determination is based on who controls the settlement decision. If the employee controlled the choice, it would generally be classified as a liability (because the entity could be required to transfer cash).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-14により、企業が現金決済と株式決済の選択権を持つ場合、株式発行で現金移転義務を回避できるため資本分類。決済方法の選択権が誰にあるかが判定の鍵。従業員が選択権を持つ場合は一般に負債分類。

questions.stem: Entity N cancels existing underwater options and simultaneously grants replacement options with a lower exercise price. The fair value of the original options at cancellation is $2 per option and the fair value of the new options is $9 per option. 5,000 options were cancelled and replaced. What is the incremental compensation cost?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0 because the original options were underwater

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-20-35-7, a cancellation of an existing award accompanied by the concurrent grant of a replacement award is treated as a modification. The incremental fair value = $9 − $2 = $7 per option. Total incremental cost = 5,000 × $7 = $35,000. Additionally, any unrecognized compensation from the original award must continue to be recognized. The total cost going forward = unrecognized original cost + $35,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-7により、既存報酬の取消と代替報酬の同時付与は修正として扱う。増分公正価値 = $9 − $2 = $7/個。増分費用 = 5,000 × $7 = $35,000。元の未認識報酬も引き続き認識。

questions.stem: Entity P grants options to an employee on January 1, Year 1 with a stated vesting period of 4 years. However, the employee is retirement-eligible on January 1, Year 3. Under ASC 718, what is the requisite service period?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 4 years (the stated vesting period)

choices.label: B
choices.text: 2 years (until retirement eligibility)

choices.label: C
choices.text: 1 year

choices.label: D
choices.text: The remaining expected service life of the employee

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-55-10, when an employee can retain an unvested award upon retirement (the award does not require forfeiture upon retirement), the requisite service period is the shorter of the stated vesting period or the period to retirement eligibility. If the employee becomes retirement-eligible before the end of the stated vesting period, compensation cost is accelerated over the period from grant date to retirement eligibility date (2 years).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-55-10により、退職時に未確定報酬が失効しない場合、必要勤務期間は明示的権利確定期間と退職適格日までの期間の短い方。退職適格が権利確定前のため、付与日から退職適格日までの2年間で費用認識を前倒し。

questions.stem: Under what circumstances may an entity use the simplified method to estimate the expected term of a stock option?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the entity has sufficient historical exercise data for all option grants

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the entity lacks sufficient historical exercise data to provide a reasonable estimate and the option is a 'plain vanilla' option

choices.label: C
choices.text: For any stock option granted to employees, regardless of data availability

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only for nonpublic entities

questions.explanation: Under SEC Staff Accounting Bulletin No. 107 (and SAB 110), the simplified method calculates expected term as (vesting period + contractual term) / 2. It is available when the entity lacks sufficient historical exercise data and the options meet the 'plain vanilla' criteria: granted at-the-money, exercisability conditioned on service only, employees terminate → short exercise period, and the option is non-transferable and non-hedgeable.
questions.explanationJa: SAB 107（及びSAB 110）により、簡便法は十分な行使履歴データがなく、オプションが「プレーンバニラ」条件を満たす場合に使用可能。期待期間 = (権利確定期間 + 契約期間) ÷ 2。条件：ATM付与、勤務条件のみ、非譲渡・非ヘッジ可能。

questions.stem: An employee's stock options are fully vested but expire unexercised because the stock price never exceeds the exercise price during the post-vesting exercise period. What happens to the previously recognized compensation expense?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The compensation expense is reversed

choices.label: B
choices.text: No reversal; the compensation expense remains as recognized

choices.label: C
choices.text: 50% of the expense is reversed

choices.label: D
choices.text: The expense is reclassified from APIC to Retained Earnings

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-35-9, previously recognized compensation cost for equity-classified awards is not reversed if the employee has satisfied the service condition and the option expires unexercised (post-vesting cancellation). The grant-date fair value reflected the possibility of expiration, so no adjustment is needed. The APIC balance from the compensation remains in equity. Only pre-vesting forfeitures can result in cost reversals.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-9により、勤務条件を満たした後にオプションが未行使で失効しても、以前認識した報酬費用は戻し入れない。付与日公正価値に失効の可能性が反映済み。APICの残高は資本に残る。権利確定前の失効のみ費用戻入が可能。

questions.stem: Entity R grants stock options with a clawback provision that requires the employee to return shares if terminated for cause within 2 years after vesting. How does the clawback affect compensation cost recognition?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Compensation cost is recognized over the vesting period; the clawback period does not extend the requisite service period

choices.label: B
choices.text: Compensation cost is deferred until the clawback period expires

choices.label: C
choices.text: The clawback extends the requisite service period by 2 years

choices.label: D
choices.text: No compensation is recognized until the clawback expires

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-55-4, a clawback provision that is contingent on termination for cause (a non-substantive condition that is unlikely to occur absent misconduct) does not extend the requisite service period. Compensation cost is recognized over the original vesting period. If a clawback is actually triggered, the reversal is accounted for in the period of the forfeiture event. The clawback does not change the grant-date fair value.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-55-4により、不正行為を条件とするクローバック条項（実質的でない条件）は必要勤務期間を延長しない。報酬費用は元の権利確定期間で認識。クローバックが実際に発動された場合、その期間に失効処理を行う。

questions.stem: Entity S has RSUs that vest for an employee. The company withholds 40% of the shares (37 shares out of 100) to satisfy the employee's tax withholding obligation and remits the equivalent cash to the tax authority. The remaining 63 shares are delivered to the employee. How should the withheld shares be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As treasury stock, with cash credited

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a reduction to APIC, with a payable to the tax authority

choices.label: C
choices.text: As compensation expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: The withholding has no accounting impact if it does not exceed the maximum statutory rate

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-18A (as amended by ASU 2016-09), share withholding up to the maximum statutory tax rate does not cause liability classification of the award. The cash payment to the tax authority is recorded as a reduction to equity (typically APIC), with a corresponding payable. Entry: Debit APIC for the cash equivalent of withheld shares, Credit Cash (or Tax Payable). The shares are never issued to the employee, so no treasury stock is recorded.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-18A（ASU 2016-09改正）により、法定最高税率以内の源泉徴収は報酬の負債分類を引き起こさない。税務当局への現金支払はAPIC（資本）の減額として記録。源泉分の株式は従業員に発行されないため自己株式は計上しない。

questions.stem: Entity T grants options with BOTH a service condition (3 years) and a market condition (stock price must exceed $100). The Monte Carlo model values the option at $12 incorporating the market condition. If the stock never reaches $100 but the employee completes 3 years of service, what total compensation is recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $0 because the market condition was not met

choices.label: B
choices.text: The full amount based on the $12 grant-date fair value

choices.label: C
choices.text: 50% of the grant-date fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: The intrinsic value at the end of the service period

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-30-14, the effect of a market condition is reflected in the grant-date fair value and is not subsequently adjusted regardless of whether the market condition is met. The $12 fair value (from Monte Carlo) already discounts for the probability of achieving the $100 target. If the service condition is met (3 years served), the full compensation based on $12 per option is recognized. Only failure to meet the service condition would result in reversal.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-14により、市場条件は付与日公正価値に反映され、条件達成の有無にかかわらず調整しない。$12はモンテカルロで$100到達確率を反映済み。勤務条件（3年）を満たせば$12ベースの全額を認識。勤務条件未達のみ戻入。

questions.stem: Entity U grants stock options to its non-employee board members as compensation for their board service. Under ASC 718, how are these awards accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Measured at the fair value of the services received

choices.label: B
choices.text: Measured at the grant-date fair value of the options, using the same framework as employee awards

choices.label: C
choices.text: Not within the scope of ASC 718

choices.label: D
choices.text: Measured at intrinsic value on each reporting date

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-15-4 (as amended by ASU 2018-07), share-based payments to nonemployees (including non-employee directors) are measured and recognized using the same framework as employee awards — at the grant-date fair value of the equity instruments. The previous distinction requiring nonemployee awards to be measured at the fair value of services received was eliminated. Directors providing board services fall within the scope of ASC 718.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-15-4（ASU 2018-07改正）により、非従業員取締役への株式報酬も従業員報酬と同じ枠組みで、付与日の持分商品の公正価値で測定・認識する。以前の「受領サービスの公正価値で測定」の区別は廃止。

questions.stem: Entity A granted 8,000 stock options with a grant-date fair value of $10 per option. All options vested, and the company recognized total compensation expense of $80,000 (credited to APIC - Stock Options). The options expired unexercised because the stock price remained below the exercise price. What journal entry, if any, should Entity A record upon expiration?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Compensation Expense $80,000; Credit Cash $80,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit APIC - Stock Options $80,000; Credit Compensation Expense $80,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: No journal entry is required

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit APIC - Stock Options $80,000; Credit APIC $80,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-35-9, when fully vested equity-classified stock options expire unexercised, no journal entry is required. The compensation expense previously recognized is not reversed, because the employee fulfilled the service condition and the grant-date fair value already incorporated the probability of expiration. The APIC - Stock Options balance remains in equity. Some entities may choose to reclassify the balance within equity (from APIC - Stock Options to APIC), but this is not required and has no income statement effect. The correct answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-9により、権利確定済みの持分分類ストックオプションが未行使で失効した場合、仕訳は不要。報酬費用は従業員が勤務条件を満たしており、付与日公正価値に失効確率が反映済みのため戻し入れない。APIC残高は資本に留まる。企業によってはAPIC内で振替を行うこともあるが、必須ではなく損益影響もない。

questions.stem: An employee receives 5,000 shares of restricted stock on January 1, Year 1 (fair value $20/share) that vest over 4 years. The employee makes a Section 83(b) election for tax purposes. Which of the following best describes the tax and GAAP implications?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The Section 83(b) election accelerates both the tax deduction and GAAP compensation expense to the grant date

choices.label: B
choices.text: The Section 83(b) election accelerates the employee's taxable income to the grant date, but GAAP expense is still recognized over the 4-year vesting period

choices.label: C
choices.text: The Section 83(b) election has no effect on either tax or GAAP recognition

choices.label: D
choices.text: The Section 83(b) election defers both the tax deduction and GAAP expense to the vesting date

questions.explanation: A Section 83(b) election causes the employee to recognize taxable income at the grant date (based on the grant-date fair value of the stock) rather than waiting until the vesting date. However, ASC 718 requires that GAAP compensation expense be recognized over the requisite service period (4-year vesting period) regardless of the employee's tax election. The employer's corresponding tax deduction also occurs at the grant date when the employee makes the 83(b) election, creating a temporary difference. But the GAAP expense recognition pattern is unaffected. The correct answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: Section 83(b)選択により、従業員の課税所得は付与日に認識される（確定日ではなく）。しかしASC 718のGAAP上の報酬費用は83(b)選択にかかわらず、4年間の必要勤務期間にわたり認識する。雇用主の税務上の控除も付与日に発生し一時差異が生じるが、GAAP費用の認識パターンには影響しない。

questions.stem: Entity B grants 3,000 RSUs to an employee on January 1, Year 1. The RSUs vest after 2 years of service, but the employee must hold the resulting shares for an additional 1 year after vesting (a post-vesting holding period). The grant-date fair value is $25 per RSU. Over what period should compensation expense be recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 3 years (vesting period plus holding period)

choices.label: B
choices.text: 2 years (the vesting period only)

choices.label: C
choices.text: 1 year (the holding period only)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Immediately at the grant date

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-1, compensation cost is recognized over the requisite service period. A post-vesting holding period (also called a restriction on sale) does not extend the requisite service period because the employee is not required to provide additional service during the holding period. The restriction affects only the liquidity of the shares, not the vesting. The holding period is reflected in the grant-date fair value (as a discount for illiquidity) but does not change the recognition period. The correct answer is B: 2-year vesting period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-1により、報酬費用は必要勤務期間にわたり認識する。権利確定後の保有期間（売却制限期間）は、追加の勤務を求めるものではないため、必要勤務期間を延長しない。保有制限は株式の流動性にのみ影響し、付与日公正価値に流動性ディスカウントとして反映される。費用認識期間は2年間の権利確定期間のみ。

questions.stem: Entity C grants 12,000 stock options on January 1, Year 1 with a grant-date fair value of $6 per option. The options vest in three equal annual installments (4,000 each year). Entity C elects to use the straight-line method over the entire 3-year vesting period (rather than treating each tranche separately). What is the compensation expense in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Total compensation cost = 12,000 options x $6 = $72,000. When an entity elects the straight-line method for graded vesting awards (applying straight-line over the longest vesting period for the entire award), Year 1 expense = $72,000 / 3 years = $24,000. This differs from the accelerated method (treating each tranche separately), which would front-load more expense in Year 1. ASC 718-10-35-8 permits either approach as an accounting policy election, but requires at least as much expense as straight-line over the longest tranche in each interim period. The correct answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: 報酬総額 = 12,000 x $6 = $72,000。段階的権利確定で全体に定額法を適用する場合、Year 1費用 = $72,000 ÷ 3年 = $24,000。各トランチを別個に扱う加速法ではYear 1に多く費用計上されるが、ASC 718-10-35-8はどちらの方法も会計方針として許容している（ただし各中間期で最低限の費用認識基準あり）。

questions.stem: Entity D grants 10,000 performance share units (PSUs) that vest based on total shareholder return (TSR) relative to a peer group over 3 years. The grant-date fair value, determined using a Monte Carlo simulation incorporating the TSR market condition, is $18 per unit. At the end of Year 3, Entity D's TSR ranks in the bottom quartile and the PSUs do not vest. What is the total compensation expense recognized over the 3-year period?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $0 because the TSR condition was not met

choices.label: D
choices.text: $60,000 per year, adjusted for TSR achievement

questions.explanation: TSR is a market condition under ASC 718 because it depends on the entity's stock price performance. Under ASC 718-10-30-14, the effect of a market condition is incorporated into the grant-date fair value via the Monte Carlo simulation and is not subsequently revised regardless of whether the condition is met. Total compensation = 10,000 x $18 = $180,000, recognized ratably over the 3-year service period ($60,000 per year). Even though the TSR condition was not met and the PSUs did not vest, the full $180,000 is recognized because market condition outcomes do not trigger reversal. The correct answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: TSRは株価パフォーマンスに依存するため、ASC 718の市場条件に該当する。ASC 718-10-30-14により、市場条件はモンテカルロシミュレーションで付与日公正価値に織り込まれ、条件達成の有無にかかわらず事後修正しない。報酬総額 = 10,000 x $18 = $180,000を3年間で均等認識（年$60,000）。TSR未達でPSUが確定しなくても全額$180,000を認識する。

questions.stem: Before the adoption of ASU 2018-07, how did the measurement of share-based payments to nonemployees differ from those to employees under U.S. GAAP?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Nonemployee awards were measured at intrinsic value; employee awards at fair value

choices.label: B
choices.text: Nonemployee awards were measured at fair value on the performance completion date with periodic remeasurement; employee awards were measured once at the grant date

choices.label: C
choices.text: There was no difference; both were always measured at grant-date fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: Nonemployee awards were measured at the lower of cost or fair value

questions.explanation: Before ASU 2018-07, nonemployee share-based payments were governed by ASC 505-50, which required measurement at fair value of the equity instruments at the earlier of (1) the date a commitment for performance was reached or (2) the date performance was complete. Awards were remeasured at each reporting date until the measurement date. ASU 2018-07 eliminated this distinction by bringing nonemployee awards into ASC 718, aligning measurement with employee awards at the grant-date fair value with no subsequent remeasurement for equity-classified awards. The correct answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2018-07以前、非従業員への株式報酬はASC 505-50に基づき、業績完了日（または業績コミットメント日）の公正価値で測定し、測定日まで各報告日に再測定を要した。従業員報酬は付与日に一度測定のみ。ASU 2018-07でこの区別が廃止され、非従業員報酬もASC 718の枠組みで付与日公正価値で測定するよう統一された。

questions.subtopic: Stock Compensation - Related Tax Deduction Timing
questions.stem: Entity E grants nonqualified stock options (NQSOs) to an employee. For tax purposes, when does the employer receive a deduction for NQSOs?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the grant date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Ratably over the vesting period

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the exercise date, based on the intrinsic value at exercise

choices.label: D
choices.text: At the vesting date, based on the grant-date fair value

questions.explanation: For NQSOs, the employer receives a tax deduction at the time the employee exercises the option, equal to the intrinsic value at exercise (market price minus exercise price). This creates a temporary difference with GAAP, which recognizes compensation expense based on the grant-date fair value over the vesting period. The difference between the cumulative GAAP expense and the tax deduction at exercise gives rise to either an excess tax benefit (if the tax deduction exceeds book expense) or a tax deficiency (if it is less). The correct answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: NQSOの場合、雇用主の税務上の控除は従業員がオプションを行使した時点で、行使時の本源的価値（市場価格−行使価格）に基づいて発生する。GAAP上は付与日公正価値を権利確定期間で認識するため、一時差異が生じる。税務控除とGAAP費用の差額により、超過税務便益または税務不足額が発生する。

questions.stem: Entity F grants 10,000 stock options on January 1, Year 1 (grant-date fair value $9/option, 2-year cliff vest, 10% estimated forfeiture rate). At the end of Year 1, Entity F revises its forfeiture estimate to 4%. What is the total compensation expense recognized in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-35-3, changes in forfeiture estimates result in a cumulative catch-up adjustment. Original total compensation = 10,000 x $9 x (1 - 10%) = $81,000. Original Year 1 expense would have been $81,000 / 2 = $40,500. At Year 1 end, the revised total = 10,000 x $9 x (1 - 4%) = $86,400. Revised cumulative through Year 1 = $86,400 x (1/2) = $43,200. Since this is the first year, all $43,200 is recognized in Year 1 (cumulative catch-up from $0). Choice A ($40,500) uses the original 10% rate. Choice C ($45,000) ignores forfeitures. Choice D ($36,000) uses a 20% forfeiture rate. The correct answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-3により、没収率見積りの変更は累積的キャッチアップ調整を行う。原見積り報酬総額 = 10,000 x $9 x (1 - 10%) = $81,000。修正後報酬総額 = 10,000 x $9 x (1 - 4%) = $86,400。Year 1末までの累計 = $86,400 x 1/2 = $43,200。初年度のため$43,200全額をYear 1で認識。選択肢A($40,500)は原10%率、C($45,000)は没収率無視、D($36,000)は20%率の誤り。

questions.stem: Entity G grants performance share units (PSUs) with a target award of 10,000 shares. The payout ranges from 0% to 200% of target based on EPS achievement. The grant-date fair value is $30 per share. At the end of Year 1 (of a 3-year performance period), management estimates 150% achievement is probable. What is the Year 1 compensation expense?
questions.explanation: EPS is a performance condition. At 150% achievement, the expected payout is 10,000 x 150% = 15,000 shares. Total expected compensation = 15,000 x $30 = $450,000. Year 1 expense = $450,000 x 1/3 = $150,000. Under ASC 718-10-25-20, compensation cost for performance conditions is recognized when the outcome is probable, based on the best estimate of the number of awards expected to vest. The correct answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: EPSは業績条件。150%達成見込みで、予想支給数 = 10,000 x 150% = 15,000株。予想報酬総額 = 15,000 x $30 = $450,000。Year 1費用 = $450,000 x 1/3 = $150,000。ASC 718-10-25-20により、業績条件の報酬は達成確度に基づき最良の見積りで費用認識する。

questions.stem: Entity H grants 4,000 phantom stock units to an employee on January 1, Year 1 with a 3-year cliff vesting period. The fair value per unit is: $20 at grant date, $25 at end of Year 1, $30 at end of Year 2, and $35 at end of Year 3 (vesting date). What is the total liability recognized at the end of Year 2?
questions.explanation: Phantom stock settled in cash is liability-classified under ASC 718-30-35-3 and remeasured at fair value each reporting date. The cumulative liability at any point = fair value per unit x number of units x (elapsed service / total service period). At end of Year 2: liability = 4,000 x $30 x (2/3) = $80,000. Year 1 liability was 4,000 x $25 x (1/3) = $33,333. Year 2 expense = $80,000 - $33,333 = $46,667. The correct answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: 現金決済のファントムストックはASC 718-30-35-3により負債分類で毎期再測定。Year 2末の累計負債 = 4,000 x $30 x (2/3) = $80,000。Year 1末の負債は4,000 x $25 x (1/3) = $33,333。Year 2費用 = $80,000 - $33,333 = $46,667。

questions.stem: Entity I grants tandem awards where an employee can choose to exercise either a stock option OR a stock appreciation right (SAR), but not both. If the employee exercises the SAR, the option is cancelled, and vice versa. The SAR can be settled only in cash. How should this tandem award be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As equity, because the stock option component allows equity settlement

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a liability, because the employee has the ability to demand cash settlement through the SAR

choices.label: C
choices.text: Bifurcated: the option as equity and the SAR as a liability

choices.label: D
choices.text: As equity until the employee makes a choice, then reclassified

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-14 and ASC 718-10-25-15, when an employee has the choice between cash and equity settlement (as in a tandem SAR/option), the award is classified as a liability because the entity may be obligated to transfer cash if the employee elects the SAR. The employee's ability to demand cash settlement drives liability classification regardless of the option component. The entire tandem award is classified as a liability and remeasured at fair value each reporting period. The correct answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-14及び25-15により、従業員が現金決済（SAR）と株式決済（オプション）の選択権を持つタンデム報酬は、従業員が現金支払を要求できるため負債に分類される。従業員の現金決済選択権が負債分類を決定し、オプション要素にかかわらずタンデム報酬全体が負債として各期に公正価値で再測定される。

questions.subtopic: Stock Compensation - Dividends on Unvested Shares
questions.stem: Entity J grants 6,000 shares of restricted stock to an employee. During the vesting period, the company pays dividends of $2 per share on all outstanding shares, including the unvested restricted shares. How should Entity J account for dividends paid on the unvested restricted shares?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a reduction of retained earnings (same as dividends on any other outstanding share)

choices.label: B
choices.text: As additional compensation expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a reduction of APIC

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a prepaid asset until the shares vest

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-55-47, dividends paid on unvested restricted shares that are not expected to be returned if the employee forfeits the award (nonforfeitable dividends) are charged to retained earnings like regular dividends. However, if the restricted stock award is expected to be forfeited, dividends paid are treated as additional compensation expense. More commonly, if dividends are forfeitable (returned upon forfeiture), they are treated as additional compensation expense. The standard treatment for dividends on unvested shares where the award has a service condition is to recognize them as compensation expense. The correct answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-55-47により、未確定の制限付株式に対する配当の処理は没収可能性に依存する。配当が没収時に返還される場合（没収可能配当）、追加の報酬費用として認識する。勤務条件付の未確定株式への配当は一般に報酬費用として処理される。

questions.stem: Entity K grants 15,000 stock options on January 1, Year 1 with a grant-date fair value of $8 per option and a 4-year cliff vest. Entity K prepares quarterly financial statements. What compensation expense should be reported in the Q1 Year 1 interim financial statements?
questions.explanation: Total compensation = 15,000 x $8 = $120,000 over 4 years (16 quarters). Under ASC 718-10-35-2 and ASC 270 (interim reporting), compensation expense is recognized ratably over the requisite service period, including in interim periods. Q1 Year 1 expense = $120,000 / 16 quarters = $7,500. Alternatively: annual expense = $120,000 / 4 = $30,000; quarterly = $30,000 / 4 = $7,500. The correct answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: 報酬総額 = 15,000 x $8 = $120,000を4年間（16四半期）で認識。ASC 718-10-35-2及びASC 270（中間報告）により、必要勤務期間にわたり均等に認識。Q1 Year 1費用 = $120,000 ÷ 16四半期 = $7,500。年間費用$30,000 ÷ 4四半期 = $7,500でも同じ。

questions.stem: Which of the following is an advantage of the binomial (lattice) model over the Black-Scholes model for valuing employee stock options under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The binomial model requires fewer assumptions and inputs

choices.label: B
choices.text: The binomial model can incorporate the possibility of early exercise at various stock price levels over the option term

choices.label: C
choices.text: The binomial model always produces a lower fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: The binomial model is required for all public companies

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-55-16, the binomial (lattice) model has the advantage of modeling the possibility of early exercise at various points during the option's life based on different stock price levels. The Black-Scholes model uses a single expected term assumption and cannot model the interaction between stock price movements and exercise behavior over time. Neither model is required — ASC 718 allows any model that meets fair value measurement objectives. The lattice model generally requires more inputs, not fewer. The correct answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-55-16により、二項（格子）モデルはオプション期間中の様々な株価水準での早期行使の可能性をモデル化できる利点がある。Black-Scholesモデルは単一の予想期間を使用し、株価変動と行使行動の相互作用をモデル化できない。ASC 718はどちらのモデルも要求しない。格子モデルは一般にインプットが多く必要。

questions.subtopic: Monte Carlo Simulation for Market Conditions
questions.stem: When is a Monte Carlo simulation most appropriate for valuing stock compensation awards under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the award has only a service condition

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the award has a performance condition based on revenue targets

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the award has a market condition such as a stock price target or relative TSR

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the entity is a nonpublic company with no trading history

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-55-15 and 718-10-55-16, Monte Carlo simulation is most appropriate when the award contains market conditions (such as stock price targets, relative TSR, or stock price hurdles) because it can model the probability of achieving various stock price outcomes and incorporate the correlation between the entity's stock and the market or peer group. Service conditions do not require complex modeling. Performance conditions based on operating metrics (like revenue) are not reflected in fair value — they affect the number of awards expected to vest. The correct answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-55-15及び55-16により、モンテカルロシミュレーションは株価目標や相対TSRなどの市場条件を含む報酬の評価に最も適している。様々な株価結果の確率と企業株価と市場/同業他社の相関をモデル化できる。勤務条件は複雑なモデルを要しない。売上高等の業績条件は公正価値に反映せず確定見込数で調整する。

questions.subtopic: Compensation Expense - Total Over Vesting Period
questions.stem: Entity L grants 25,000 stock options on January 1, Year 1 with a grant-date fair value of $12 per option and a 5-year cliff vest. The estimated forfeiture rate is 8%. What is the TOTAL compensation expense to be recognized over the vesting period?
questions.explanation: Total compensation expense = number of options x fair value per option x (1 - forfeiture rate) = 25,000 x $12 x (1 - 0.08) = 25,000 x $12 x 0.92 = $276,000. This amount is recognized ratably over the 5-year vesting period ($55,200 per year). Choice B ($300,000) ignores forfeitures. Choice C ($324,000) incorrectly adds the forfeiture rate. Choice D ($240,000) uses a 20% forfeiture rate. The correct answer is A.

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT a required disclosure under ASC 718 for share-based payment arrangements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A description of the share-based payment arrangements

choices.label: B
choices.text: The weighted-average grant-date fair value of options granted during the period

choices.label: C
choices.text: The names and positions of individual employees who received stock options

choices.label: D
choices.text: The total compensation cost recognized and the total tax benefit recognized

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-50, required disclosures include: (1) a description of the arrangements, (2) the weighted-average grant-date fair value, (3) total compensation cost and related tax benefits, (4) a rollforward of option activity, and (5) the method and assumptions used to estimate fair value. ASC 718 does NOT require disclosure of specific individual employees by name and position — this would be a proxy statement or SEC executive compensation disclosure matter, not a financial statement disclosure under ASC 718. The correct answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-50の開示要求は、(1)報酬制度の記述、(2)加重平均付与日公正価値、(3)報酬費用総額と税務便益、(4)オプション活動のロールフォワード、(5)公正価値見積りの方法と仮定を含む。個別従業員の氏名・役職は要求されない（これはプロキシステートメント/SEC報酬開示の範囲）。

questions.stem: Entity M grants stock options with a reload feature: when an employee exercises options by tendering previously owned shares, the employee automatically receives new replacement options equal to the number of shares tendered, at the current market price. How is the reload feature accounted for under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The reload feature is ignored and only the original grant is valued

choices.label: B
choices.text: The reload feature is incorporated into the fair value of the original option at the grant date

choices.label: C
choices.text: The reload option is treated as a new grant and measured at its own grant-date fair value when issued

choices.label: D
choices.text: The reload feature is treated as a modification of the original award

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-5, a reload option (an option automatically granted when shares are tendered to exercise an existing option) is treated as a new, separate award. The reload option is measured at its own grant-date fair value at the time it is granted (i.e., when the original options are exercised), not when the original options were granted. The reload feature is NOT incorporated into the fair value of the original award and is NOT treated as a modification. The correct answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-5により、リロードオプション（既存オプション行使時に自動付与される新オプション）は新たな独立した付与として扱われる。リロードオプションは発行時（原オプション行使時）に自身の付与日公正価値で測定される。原オプションの公正価値には含めず、修正としても扱わない。

questions.stem: Entity N grants 20,000 stock options on January 1, Year 1 (grant-date fair value $9/option, 3-year cliff vest). The initial forfeiture estimate is 5%. During Year 2, 1,500 options are forfeited and Entity N revises the total expected forfeiture rate to 12%. What is the cumulative compensation expense through Year 2?
questions.explanation: Under the estimated forfeiture method, actual forfeitures are not tracked individually; instead, the revised forfeiture rate is applied to the total grant. Revised total compensation = 20,000 x $9 x (1 - 12%) = 20,000 x $9 x 0.88 = $158,400. Cumulative through Year 2 = $158,400 x 2/3 = $105,600. Choice B ($114,000) uses a 5% rate: 20,000 x $9 x 0.95 x 2/3 = $114,000. Choice C ($120,000) ignores forfeitures entirely. The correct answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: 見積没収法では、実際の没収を個別追跡せず修正後の没収率を付与総数に適用する。修正後の報酬総額 = 20,000 x $9 x (1 - 12%) = 20,000 x $9 x 0.88 = $158,400。Year 2までの累計 = $158,400 x 2/3 = $105,600。選択肢B($114,000)は5%率を使用、C($120,000)は没収率無視。

questions.subtopic: Restricted Stock Award vs RSU Comparison
questions.stem: Which of the following correctly distinguishes restricted stock awards (RSAs) from restricted stock units (RSUs) under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: RSAs are issued as actual shares at grant; RSUs represent a promise to deliver shares at vesting

choices.label: B
choices.text: RSAs are always liability-classified; RSUs are always equity-classified

choices.label: C
choices.text: RSAs vest immediately; RSUs require a service period

choices.label: D
choices.text: RSAs and RSUs are accounted for identically with no practical differences

questions.explanation: The key distinction is that restricted stock awards (RSAs) involve the actual issuance of shares at the grant date, subject to forfeiture if vesting conditions are not met. The employee is a shareholder of record and typically has voting and dividend rights. Restricted stock units (RSUs) are a contractual promise to deliver shares (or cash) at a future date upon vesting; no shares are issued until vesting. Both can be equity or liability classified depending on settlement terms. Both can have various vesting conditions. The correct answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: 主な違いは、制限付株式(RSA)は付与日に実際の株式が発行され（権利確定条件未達時は没収）、従業員は記録上の株主として議決権・配当権を持つ。制限付株式ユニット(RSU)は権利確定時に株式（又は現金）を交付する契約上の約束で、確定まで株式は発行されない。どちらも決済条件により持分・負債分類がありうる。

questions.subtopic: Share-Based Payment - Cash vs Equity Classification Test
questions.stem: Entity P grants SARs to an employee. The terms state that the employee chooses whether to receive cash or shares upon exercise. Under ASC 718, how should this award be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As equity, because share settlement is an option

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a liability, because the employee can demand cash

choices.label: C
choices.text: As equity if the entity expects the employee to choose shares

choices.label: D
choices.text: Bifurcated between liability and equity components

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-15, when the employee holds the right to choose between cash and equity settlement, the award is classified as a liability. This is because the entity may be required to settle in cash at the employee's election, creating an obligation. The entity's expectation of the employee's choice is irrelevant — the classification is based on the contractual terms. This contrasts with the case where the entity holds the settlement choice, which results in equity classification. The correct answer is B.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-15により、従業員が現金決済と株式決済の選択権を持つ場合、企業は従業員の要求により現金で決済する義務が生じうるため負債に分類する。企業の予想は無関係で、契約条件に基づいて分類。企業が選択権を持つ場合は持分分類となる点と対照的。

questions.subtopic: Stock Compensation - IPO or Private Company Transition
questions.stem: A private company that previously used the intrinsic value method for measuring equity-classified stock options goes through an IPO and becomes a public entity. What happens to its stock compensation accounting?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The company must retrospectively restate all prior periods using fair value

choices.label: B
choices.text: The company must prospectively adopt the fair value method for new grants after the IPO, and continue intrinsic value for existing grants

choices.label: C
choices.text: The company must transition to the fair value method; existing awards are remeasured at the IPO date

choices.label: D
choices.text: No change is required; the company may continue using intrinsic value

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-35-19, when a nonpublic entity that elected the intrinsic value method becomes a public entity, it must transition to the fair value method. The entity remeasures all outstanding unvested awards at fair value as of the date it becomes public, recognizing any incremental compensation cost over the remaining vesting period. This is a change from the intrinsic value method to the fair value method triggered by the change in entity status. Previously vested awards are not adjusted. The correct answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-19により、本源的価値法を選択していた非公開企業が公開企業になった場合、公正価値法へ移行する。公開日時点で未確定の報酬を公正価値で再測定し、追加報酬費用を残存権利確定期間で認識。企業ステータス変更による方法変更。既に確定済みの報酬は調整しない。

questions.subtopic: Stock Option - Expected Volatility for Public Entity
questions.stem: Under ASC 718, which of the following may a public entity consider when estimating expected volatility for stock option valuation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only historical volatility of the entity's own stock

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only implied volatility from traded options on the entity's stock

choices.label: C
choices.text: Either historical volatility, implied volatility, or a combination of both

choices.label: D
choices.text: A fixed volatility rate prescribed by the SEC

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-55-36, a public entity should consider both historical volatility and implied volatility (from exchange-traded options) when estimating expected volatility. The entity may use historical volatility alone, implied volatility alone, or a combination of both, depending on which approach best estimates the expected volatility over the expected term of the employee stock option. There is no prescribed rate. The correct answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-55-36により、公開企業は予想ボラティリティの見積りにヒストリカル・ボラティリティとインプライド・ボラティリティ（上場オプションから算出）の両方を考慮すべきである。いずれか単独または両方の組み合わせを使用でき、オプション予想期間の期待ボラティリティを最もよく見積る方法を選択する。規定税率はない。

questions.stem: Entity Q has 30,000 outstanding unvested stock options with a remaining vesting period of 2 years. The original grant-date fair value was $10 per option with $180,000 of unrecognized compensation. Entity Q accelerates vesting so that all options vest immediately. The fair value immediately before and after the modification is $7 per option (no change). What is the accounting effect?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No additional cost; the remaining $180,000 is recognized immediately

choices.label: B
choices.text: Additional compensation of $90,000 is recognized

choices.label: C
choices.text: The original $300,000 total is reversed and remeasured at $210,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No accounting effect because the fair value did not change

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-20-35-6, when vesting is accelerated in a modification, the entity recognizes any remaining unrecognized compensation cost in the period of the modification. Since the fair value did not change (no incremental cost), there is no additional compensation beyond the original grant-date amount. The unrecognized balance of $180,000 is recognized immediately upon acceleration.
