Total compensation over the life of the award remains the original grant-date fair value amount ($300,000 = 30,000 x $10). The correct answer is A.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-6により、権利確定を加速する修正では、未認識残高を修正期間に即時認識する。公正価値に変更がないため（増分費用なし）、元の付与日公正価値を超える追加報酬はない。未認識残高$180,000を即時認識。報酬総額は原付与日公正価値($300,000 = 30,000 x $10)のまま。

questions.stem: Which of the following changes was introduced by ASU 2016-09 (Improvements to Employee Share-Based Payment Accounting)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Excess tax benefits and deficiencies are now recognized in APIC rather than in the income statement

choices.label: B
choices.text: Entities are now required to estimate forfeitures; the actual forfeiture method is no longer permitted

choices.label: C
choices.text: Excess tax benefits and deficiencies are now recognized in the income statement, and entities may elect to account for forfeitures as they occur

choices.label: D
choices.text: All stock compensation must now be measured at intrinsic value

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-09 made several simplifying changes to ASC 718: (1) All excess tax benefits and deficiencies are recognized as income tax expense in the income statement (previously, excess benefits were recorded in APIC). (2) Entities may make an accounting policy election to recognize forfeitures as they occur (actual method) instead of estimating forfeitures. (3) Cash paid for tax withholding is classified as a financing activity on the statement of cash flows. (4) The threshold for liability classification due to tax withholding was raised to the maximum statutory rate. The correct answer is C.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09はASC 718を簡素化した：(1)超過税務便益・不足額を全額損益計算書の法人税等で認識（従前はAPIC計上）。(2)没収率の見積り法に加え、実際発生時認識法の選択が可能に。(3)源泉徴収の現金支出はキャッシュフロー計算書で財務活動に分類。(4)源泉徴収による負債分類の閾値を法定最高税率に引き上げ。

questions.subtopic: Grant Date - Mutual Understanding and Approval
questions.stem: On December 15, Year 1, a company's compensation committee approves a stock option award and communicates the key terms to the recipient employee, who accepts them the same day. The award documents are formally executed on January 10, Year 2. The service period begins January 1, Year 2. Under ASC 718, which date is the grant date for measuring fair value?

choices.label: A
choices.text: December 15, Year 1

choices.label: B
choices.text: January 1, Year 2

choices.label: C
choices.text: January 10, Year 2

choices.label: D
choices.text: December 31, Year 1

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-20 defines the grant date as the date at which the employer and employee reach a mutual understanding of the key terms and conditions of a share-based payment award, the employer becomes contingently obligated, the employee begins to benefit from (or be adversely affected by) subsequent changes in the stock price, and the award has been approved by the appropriate body (e.g., the board or compensation committee). All of these were satisfied on December 15, Year 1, when the committee approved the award and the employee accepted the communicated terms. The later formal execution of documents and the start of the service period do not establish the grant date; the mutual understanding and approval on December 15 governs.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-20は付与日を、雇用者と従業員が報酬の主要条件について相互理解に達し、雇用者が条件付き義務を負い、従業員が以後の株価変動による利益・不利益を被り始め、かつ適切な機関（取締役会や報酬委員会）が承認した日と定義する。これら全ては委員会が承認し従業員が条件を受諾したYear 1の12月15日に満たされた。後日の文書正式締結や勤務期間の開始日は付与日を構成せず、12月15日の相互理解と承認が基準となる。

questions.stem: A company sponsors a compensatory employee stock purchase plan (ESPP). An employee purchases 2,000 shares when the market price is $40 per share, paying 85% of the market price (a 15% discount) with no look-back feature. Considering only the purchase discount, what amount of compensation cost relates to the discount on this purchase?
questions.explanation: For a compensatory ESPP, the discount provided to employees represents compensation cost. The discount per share = $40 market price − $34 purchase price (85% × $40) = $6 per share. Compensation cost from the discount = 2,000 shares × $6 = $12,000. The $34 purchase price paid by the employee ($68,000 total) is recorded as proceeds to equity, not compensation. The full market value of $80,000 (2,000 × $40) overstates the compensation because the employee pays $68,000 of it. A $6 per-share discount applied to only one share would be $6, which ignores the 2,000-share quantity.
questions.explanationJa: 報酬型ESPPでは、従業員に提供される割引が報酬費用となる。1株当たり割引 = 市場価格$40 − 購入価格$34（$40の85%）= $6。割引による報酬費用 = 2,000株 × $6 = $12,000。従業員が支払う購入価格$34（合計$68,000）は資本への払込として記録され報酬ではない。市場価格全額$80,000（2,000 × $40）は従業員が$68,000を支払うため報酬を過大計上する。$6を1株だけに適用した$6は2,000株という数量を無視している。

questions.stem: A company grants performance-based options (grant-date fair value $100,000, 3-year service period). At the end of Year 1 the performance condition is probable, and $33,333 of expense is recognized. During Year 2, the company modifies the award; immediately before the modification the condition has become improbable. Under the modification guidance, what is the minimum cumulative compensation cost the company must ultimately recognize for this award, assuming the service condition is met?
questions.explanation: ASC 718-20-35-3 establishes a floor: total recognized compensation cost for a modified equity-classified award must be at least the grant-date fair value of the original award, unless the original award is not expected to vest based solely on its service condition. Here the service condition is met, so the entity cannot recognize less than the original $100,000 grant-date fair value, even though the performance condition became improbable. A type that would otherwise let the entity reverse to $0 applies only when vesting fails because of the service condition, not a performance condition that the modification addresses. The $33,333 already recognized and the $66,667 remaining together build to the $100,000 floor.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-3は下限を定める。修正された持分分類報酬の認識報酬費用総額は、勤務条件のみに基づいて確定が見込まれない場合を除き、最低でも原報酬の付与日公正価値以上でなければならない。本件では勤務条件が満たされるため、業績条件が達成見込みなしとなっても、企業は原付与日公正価値$100,000を下回って認識できない。$0まで戻し入れできるのは勤務条件不充足による失効の場合のみで、修正が対応する業績条件には適用されない。既認識$33,333と残り$66,667が合わさって$100,000の下限を構成する。

questions.subtopic: Liability Award - Total Cost Equals Cash Settled
questions.stem: A company grants 8,000 cash-settled stock appreciation rights with a grant-date fair value of $14 per SAR. The SARs vest after 2 years. At settlement (end of Year 2), the intrinsic value paid in cash to employees is $22 per SAR. Over the life of the award, what is the total compensation cost recognized?
questions.explanation: For a liability-classified award (cash-settled SARs), ASC 718-30-35-3 requires remeasurement to fair value each period until settlement, so the cumulative compensation cost recognized over the award's life equals the total cash actually paid to settle the liability. Total cost = 8,000 SARs × $22 settlement value = $176,000. The grant-date fair value of $14 (total $112,000) is only the starting estimate and does not cap the cost for a liability award; the difference is recognized through remeasurement. Half of the settlement value ($88,000) or the grant-date amount understate the final cash outflow.
questions.explanationJa: 負債分類報酬（現金決済SAR）では、ASC 718-30-35-3により決済まで毎期公正価値で再測定するため、award の存続期間で認識される累積報酬費用は負債決済のために実際に支払われた現金総額に等しい。総費用 = 8,000 SAR × 決済額$22 = $176,000。付与日公正価値$14（合計$112,000）は当初見積りにすぎず、負債報酬では費用の上限とならない。差額は再測定を通じて認識される。決済額の半分（$88,000）や付与日金額は最終的な現金流出を過小評価する。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, a company grants 6,000 stock options with a grant-date fair value of $14 per option. The options cliff-vest after 4 years of continuous service. The company accounts for forfeitures as they occur, and no forfeitures happen in Year 1. What compensation expense is recognized for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Total compensation cost = 6,000 options × $14 = $84,000. For a cliff-vesting award, ASC 718-10-35-2 recognizes cost on a straight-line basis over the 4-year requisite service period. Year 1 expense = $84,000 ÷ 4 = $21,000. Because the company recognizes forfeitures as they occur and none occurred, no forfeiture reduction applies in Year 1. The full $84,000 is the total over four years, not a single year. Half ($42,000) would assume a 2-year period and $28,000 would assume a 3-year period.
questions.explanationJa: 総報酬費用 = 6,000個 × $14 = $84,000。崖式権利確定報酬では、ASC 718-10-35-2により4年の必要勤務期間にわたり定額法で費用認識する。Year 1費用 = $84,000 ÷ 4 = $21,000。失効を発生時認識し当期に失効がないため、Year 1に失効減額は適用されない。$84,000は4年間の総額であり単年度ではない。半分の$42,000は2年、$28,000は3年を前提とした誤り。

questions.stem: A company recognized cumulative book compensation expense of $500,000 for nonqualified stock options and established a deferred tax asset at a 25% tax rate. Upon exercise, the actual tax deduction is only $300,000 because the share price barely exceeded the exercise price. The tax rate is 25%. What is the income statement effect of the tax shortfall at exercise?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $50,000 decrease to income tax expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: $50,000 increase to income tax expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: $50,000 charge to additional paid-in capital

choices.label: D
choices.text: $125,000 increase to income tax expense

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-740-35-2 (post-ASU 2016-09), all tax effects of share-based awards at settlement, including shortfalls (deficiencies), are recognized in income tax expense in the income statement. The deferred tax asset established on book expense = $500,000 × 25% = $125,000. The actual tax benefit realized = $300,000 × 25% = $75,000. The shortfall = $125,000 − $75,000 = $50,000, which increases income tax expense as the excess DTA is written off. Routing the shortfall to additional paid-in capital reflects pre-ASU 2016-09 treatment, and $125,000 is the entire DTA rather than the deficiency.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09以降のASC 718-740-35-2により、決済時の株式報酬の税効果（不足額を含む）は全額損益計算書の法人税等で認識する。帳簿費用に基づき設定した繰延税金資産 = $500,000 × 25% = $125,000。実現した実際の税務便益 = $300,000 × 25% = $75,000。不足額 = $125,000 − $75,000 = $50,000で、超過DTAの取崩しとして法人税等を増加させる。不足額をAPICに計上するのはASU 2016-09以前の処理であり、$125,000は不足額ではなくDTA全額である。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Tax Asset - Write-Off on Expiration
questions.stem: A company recognized $240,000 of cumulative book compensation expense for nonqualified options and carried a related deferred tax asset at a 25% rate. The options expired completely unexercised because the stock price stayed below the exercise price, so no tax deduction was ever realized. How is the deferred tax asset accounted for at expiration?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It is reversed against additional paid-in capital

choices.label: B
choices.text: It remains on the balance sheet because the book expense was not reversed

choices.label: C
choices.text: The $60,000 deferred tax asset is written off to income tax expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: The $240,000 deferred tax asset is written off to income tax expense

questions.explanation: The deferred tax asset = $240,000 × 25% = $60,000. When equity-classified options expire unexercised, no tax deduction is realized, so the temporary difference reverses with no offsetting tax benefit. Under ASC 718-740-35-2 (post-ASU 2016-09), the write-off of the unrealized deferred tax asset is recognized in income tax expense in the income statement. The book compensation expense itself is not reversed (the service condition was met), but the related DTA must be eliminated because the future deductible amount will never occur. The DTA equals $60,000, not the full $240,000 pretax expense, and post-ASU 2016-09 the write-off does not flow through paid-in capital.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延税金資産 = $240,000 × 25% = $60,000。持分分類オプションが未行使で失効すると税務控除は実現せず、一時差異は相殺する税務便益なしに解消する。ASU 2016-09以降のASC 718-740-35-2により、未実現繰延税金資産の取崩しは損益計算書の法人税等で認識する。帳簿上の報酬費用自体は戻し入れない（勤務条件は充足済）が、将来の損金算入額が決して発生しないため関連DTAは消去しなければならない。DTAは税引前費用$240,000全額ではなく$60,000であり、ASU 2016-09以降は払込資本を経由しない。

questions.subtopic: Statement of Cash Flows - Excess Tax Benefits
questions.stem: Following the adoption of ASU 2016-09, how are excess tax benefits from share-based payment awards presented on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As an operating activity, no longer separated out as a financing activity

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a financing activity, consistent with prior guidance

choices.label: C
choices.text: As an investing activity

choices.label: D
choices.text: They are not reported on the statement of cash flows

questions.explanation: Under ASU 2016-09, excess tax benefits are classified as operating activities on the statement of cash flows, and they are no longer separated from other income tax cash flows. Because excess tax benefits are now recognized in income tax expense within the income statement, they naturally flow through operating activities. Prior to ASU 2016-09, excess tax benefits were presented as a financing inflow with an offsetting operating outflow. Note that cash paid to taxing authorities for shares withheld to satisfy an employee's tax obligation is classified as a financing activity, a separate item from excess tax benefits.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-09により、超過税務便益はキャッシュフロー計算書で営業活動に分類され、他の法人税キャッシュフローと区分しなくなった。超過税務便益は損益計算書の法人税等で認識されるため、自然に営業活動を通じて表示される。ASU 2016-09以前は、超過税務便益は財務活動の流入として表示され営業活動の流出で相殺されていた。なお、従業員の税負担充足のため源泉徴収した株式に係る税務当局への現金支払は財務活動に分類され、超過税務便益とは別項目である。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, a company grants 12,000 stock options with a grant-date fair value of $9 per option that vest in three equal annual installments (4,000 options each on the first, second, and third anniversaries). The company uses the accelerated (separate-tranche) attribution method. What is the compensation expense for Year 2?
questions.explanation: Under the accelerated method (ASC 718-10-35-8), each tranche is a separate award expensed straight-line over its own vesting period, with all tranches beginning at the grant date. Each tranche's total cost = 4,000 × $9 = $36,000. Year 2 expense: Tranche 1 (1-year vest) is fully expensed in Year 1, contributing $0 in Year 2. Tranche 2 (2-year vest) contributes $36,000 ÷ 2 = $18,000 in Year 2. Tranche 3 (3-year vest) contributes $36,000 ÷ 3 = $12,000 in Year 2. Year 2 total = $0 + $18,000 + $12,000 = $30,000. Year 1 (which totals $66,000) is higher because all three tranches accrue in Year 1. A straight-line approach would give $36,000 each year, which is not the accelerated method.
questions.explanationJa: 加速法（ASC 718-10-35-8）では、各トランシェを別個の報酬として自身の権利確定期間で定額認識し、全トランシェが付与日から開始する。各トランシェの総費用 = 4,000 × $9 = $36,000。Year 2費用：トランシェ1（1年確定）はYear 1で全額計上されYear 2は$0。トランシェ2（2年確定）はYear 2に$36,000 ÷ 2 = $18,000。トランシェ3（3年確定）はYear 2に$36,000 ÷ 3 = $12,000。Year 2合計 = $0 + $18,000 + $12,000 = $30,000。Year 1（合計$66,000）は全トランシェが計上されるため高い。定額法では各年$36,000となるが加速法ではない。

questions.stem: A company grants 8,000 restricted stock units on January 1, Year 1, when the stock price is $35 per unit. The RSUs cliff-vest at the end of Year 4 and will be settled in shares. The company expects all units to vest and recognizes forfeitures as they occur. What is the compensation expense for each year of the vesting period?
questions.explanation: Share-settled RSUs are equity-classified and measured at grant-date fair value, which is not remeasured (ASC 718-10-30-2). Total compensation = 8,000 units × $35 = $280,000, recognized straight-line over the 4-year requisite service period. Annual expense = $280,000 ÷ 4 = $70,000. The $280,000 is the total over all four years, not a single year. Splitting over 2 years gives $140,000 and over 3 years gives $93,333, both of which use the wrong service period.
questions.explanationJa: 株式決済RSUは持分分類で付与日公正価値により測定し再測定しない（ASC 718-10-30-2）。総報酬 = 8,000 × $35 = $280,000を4年の必要勤務期間で定額認識する。年間費用 = $280,000 ÷ 4 = $70,000。$280,000は4年間の総額であり単年度ではない。2年で割ると$140,000、3年で割ると$93,333となり、いずれも勤務期間の誤り。

questions.stem: A company has 5,000 unvested options with $30,000 of unrecognized grant-date compensation cost remaining. It reprices the options; immediately before the modification the fair value is $4 per option, and immediately after it is $11 per option. The service condition will be satisfied. What total compensation cost remains to be recognized over the remaining service period after the modification?
questions.explanation: ASC 718-20-35-3 requires the entity to continue recognizing the original award's unrecognized grant-date cost AND to add the incremental fair value from the modification. Incremental fair value = ($11 − $4) × 5,000 = $7 × 5,000 = $35,000. Remaining original cost = $30,000. Total remaining to recognize = $30,000 + $35,000 = $65,000, spread over the remaining service period. Recognizing only the $35,000 incremental amount ignores the unrecognized original cost; recognizing only $30,000 ignores the incremental value; $55,000 mistakenly uses a $5 incremental fair value.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-3は、原報酬の未認識付与日費用の認識を継続し、かつ修正による増分公正価値を加えることを要求する。増分公正価値 = ($11 − $4) × 5,000 = $7 × 5,000 = $35,000。残存原費用 = $30,000。認識すべき残存総額 = $30,000 + $35,000 = $65,000で、残存勤務期間にわたり配分する。増分$35,000のみの認識は未認識の原費用を無視し、$30,000のみは増分を無視し、$55,000は増分$5を誤用している。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, a company grants 6,000 cash-settled stock appreciation rights with a grant-date fair value of $15 each. The SARs cliff-vest after 3 years. At December 31, Year 1, the fair value per SAR has risen to $18. What is the SAR liability recognized at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Cash-settled SARs are liability-classified and remeasured to fair value each period (ASC 718-30-35-3). The liability accrues based on the current fair value and the portion of the service period elapsed. At December 31, Year 1: liability = 6,000 SARs × $18 current fair value × (1 year ÷ 3 years) = $36,000. Using the $15 grant-date fair value (giving $30,000) ignores remeasurement; the full $108,000 (6,000 × $18) ignores that only one of three years has elapsed; $54,000 incorrectly uses one-half of the service period.
questions.explanationJa: 現金決済SARは負債分類で毎期公正価値に再測定する（ASC 718-30-35-3）。負債は当期の公正価値と経過した勤務期間の割合に基づき積み上がる。Year 1末：負債 = 6,000 SAR × 当期公正価値$18 × (1年 ÷ 3年) = $36,000。付与日公正価値$15を用いると$30,000となり再測定を無視する。全額$108,000（6,000 × $18）は3年中1年のみ経過した点を無視し、$54,000は勤務期間の2分の1を誤用している。

questions.stem: Under ASC 718, the requisite service period for a share-based payment award is best described as the period during which:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The award is legally outstanding until its contractual expiration date

choices.label: B
choices.text: The employee may exercise the vested award after vesting

choices.label: C
choices.text: An employee is required to provide service in exchange for the award (the vesting period)

choices.label: D
choices.text: The entity's stock price is expected to exceed the exercise price

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-20 defines the requisite service period as the period (or periods) during which an employee is required to provide service in exchange for an award of share-based payment, which is usually the vesting period. Compensation cost is recognized over this period. The contractual term and the post-vesting exercise window extend beyond the requisite service period and do not govern expense recognition. The relationship between stock price and exercise price affects intrinsic value and fair value inputs but does not define the service period over which cost is attributed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-20は必要勤務期間を、従業員が株式報酬と引き換えに勤務を提供することが求められる期間と定義し、通常は権利確定期間である。報酬費用はこの期間にわたり認識する。契約期間や権利確定後の行使可能期間は必要勤務期間を超えて延びるが費用認識を規定しない。株価と行使価格の関係は本源的価値や公正価値のインプットに影響するが、費用を配分する勤務期間を定義しない。

questions.stem: A company grants performance share units with a target of 8,000 shares. Based on cumulative return-on-equity over a 3-year period, the payout is 0% (below threshold), 100% (at target), or 200% (at maximum). The grant-date fair value is $25 per share. At the end of Year 1, management concludes that the maximum (200%) tier is probable of being achieved. What compensation expense is recognized in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Return on equity is a performance condition, so compensation cost is based on the number of awards expected to vest at the probable outcome (ASC 718-10-25-20). At the probable 200% tier, expected shares = 8,000 × 200% = 16,000. Total expected compensation = 16,000 × $25 = $400,000, recognized over the 3-year service period. Year 1 expense = $400,000 ÷ 3 = $133,333. Using the 100% tier gives $66,667, which understates the probable outcome. The full $400,000 ignores that only one of three years has elapsed, and $200,000 reflects only the target-level total without the service-period allocation.
questions.explanationJa: 自己資本利益率は業績条件であり、報酬費用は達成見込みの結果における確定見込数に基づく（ASC 718-10-25-20）。確実な200%水準では予想株式数 = 8,000 × 200% = 16,000。予想報酬総額 = 16,000 × $25 = $400,000を3年の勤務期間で認識する。Year 1費用 = $400,000 ÷ 3 = $133,333。100%水準では$66,667となり達成見込みを過小評価する。全額$400,000は3年中1年のみ経過した点を無視し、$200,000は勤務期間配分なしの目標水準総額にすぎない。

questions.subtopic: Change in Number Expected to Vest - Catch-Up
questions.stem: A company grants 10,000 performance share units (grant-date fair value $20 per unit, 3-year service and performance period). At the end of Year 1 the performance target is probable and all 10,000 units are expected to vest. At the end of Year 2, the company revises its estimate so that only 8,000 units are now expected to vest (the target is still probable). What is the compensation expense for Year 2?
questions.explanation: Compensation cost reflects the number of awards expected to vest, updated as estimates change, with a cumulative catch-up (ASC 718-10-35-3). Year 1 expense was based on 10,000 units: 10,000 × $20 × (1 ÷ 3) = $66,667 recognized through Year 1. At the end of Year 2 the revised expectation is 8,000 units, so cumulative cost through Year 2 = 8,000 × $20 × (2 ÷ 3) = $106,667. Year 2 expense = cumulative $106,667 − prior $66,667 = $40,000. The $106,667 is the cumulative amount, not the Year 2 expense, and $66,667 is the Year 1 figure. A straight 8,000-unit one-third allocation ($53,333) ignores the catch-up from the higher Year 1 base.
questions.explanationJa: 報酬費用は確定見込数を反映し、見積り変更時に累積キャッチアップを行う（ASC 718-10-35-3）。Year 1費用は10,000ユニットに基づき10,000 × $20 × (1 ÷ 3) = $66,667をYear 1までに認識。Year 2末の修正見込みは8,000ユニットのため、Year 2末までの累積費用 = 8,000 × $20 × (2 ÷ 3) = $106,667。Year 2費用 = 累積$106,667 − 既認識$66,667 = $40,000。$106,667は累積額でありYear 2費用ではなく、$66,667はYear 1の数値。8,000ユニットを単純に3分の1配分した$53,333は高いYear 1基準からのキャッチアップを無視している。

questions.subtopic: Incentive Stock Options - No Deferred Tax Asset
questions.stem: A company grants incentive stock options (ISOs) that qualify under the Internal Revenue Code and recognizes book compensation expense over the vesting period. Assuming the employee is expected to satisfy the ISO holding-period requirements (a qualifying disposition), what deferred tax asset should the company recognize for the book compensation expense?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A deferred tax asset equal to the book expense multiplied by the tax rate

choices.label: B
choices.text: A deferred tax asset equal to the intrinsic value at exercise multiplied by the tax rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: A deferred tax asset equal to the full book compensation expense (not tax-effected)

choices.label: D
choices.text: No deferred tax asset, because a qualifying ISO does not generate a corporate tax deduction

questions.explanation: Incentive stock options that result in a qualifying disposition do not provide the employer with a tax deduction. Under ASC 718-740-25-3, compensation cost related to an award for which the employer will never receive a tax deduction does not create a deductible temporary difference, so no deferred tax asset is recognized. The book compensation expense becomes a permanent difference. A deferred tax asset would be appropriate only for awards expected to generate a future tax deduction, such as nonqualified options. Any DTA based on book expense or intrinsic value would incorrectly assume a deduction will occur.
questions.explanationJa: 適格処分となるインセンティブストックオプションは雇用主に税務控除をもたらさない。ASC 718-740-25-3により、雇用主が税務控除を決して受けない報酬に係る報酬費用は損金算入可能な一時差異を生じないため、繰延税金資産を認識しない。帳簿上の報酬費用は永久差異となる。繰延税金資産は非適格オプションなど将来の税務控除が見込まれる報酬についてのみ適切である。帳簿費用や本源的価値に基づくDTAは控除が発生すると誤って前提している。

questions.stem: A company has 80,000 stock options outstanding with an exercise price of $60. The average market price of the stock during the period is $45. Under the treasury stock method for diluted earnings per share, how many incremental shares should be added to the denominator?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 0 shares, because the options are antidilutive

choices.label: B
choices.text: 80,000 shares

choices.label: C
choices.text: 26,667 shares

choices.label: D
choices.text: 20,000 shares

questions.explanation: Under the treasury stock method, options are dilutive only when the exercise price is below the average market price (the options are in the money). Here the $60 exercise price exceeds the $45 average market price, so the options are out of the money and antidilutive. Antidilutive options are excluded entirely from the diluted EPS computation under ASC 260-10-45-17, so zero incremental shares are added. Including all 80,000 shares or computing a treasury-method increment treats the options as if they were dilutive, which would improperly reduce the dilutive effect and is not permitted for antidilutive securities.
questions.explanationJa: 自己株式方式では、行使価格が平均市場価格を下回る（インザマネー）場合にのみオプションは希薄化効果を持つ。本件では行使価格$60が平均市場価格$45を上回るため、オプションはアウトオブザマネーで反希薄化的である。反希薄化的オプションはASC 260-10-45-17により希薄化後EPS計算から完全に除外されるため、増分株式はゼロ。全80,000株を含めたり自己株式方式の増分を計算することは、オプションを希薄化的とみなすものであり、反希薄化的証券には認められない。

questions.subtopic: EPS - Assumed Proceeds Include Unrecognized Compensation
questions.stem: When applying the treasury stock method to outstanding employee stock options for diluted EPS purposes, what amounts are included in the assumed proceeds used to repurchase shares at the average market price?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Only the cash exercise price the employees would pay

choices.label: B
choices.text: The exercise price plus the average unrecognized compensation cost on the options

choices.label: C
choices.text: The exercise price plus the total intrinsic value of the options

choices.label: D
choices.text: The average market price of the shares to be issued

questions.explanation: Under ASC 260-10-45-29, the assumed proceeds in the treasury stock method for share-based payment awards include both (1) the amount the employee would pay upon exercise (the exercise price) and (2) the average unrecognized compensation cost attributed to future service. Both are treated as proceeds the entity would receive (or the benefit it would retain) and are used to buy back shares at the average market price, which reduces the net dilutive share count. Including the intrinsic value, or using the market price of the shares, is not part of the prescribed proceeds calculation.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 260-10-45-29により、株式報酬に対する自己株式方式の想定収入には、(1)行使時に従業員が支払う金額（行使価格）と(2)将来勤務に配分される平均未認識報酬費用の両方が含まれる。いずれも企業が受領する（または保持する便益となる）収入として扱われ、平均市場価格で株式を買い戻すために用いられ、純希薄化株式数を減少させる。本源的価値を含めたり、株式の市場価格を用いることは規定の収入計算の一部ではない。

questions.subtopic: EPS - Treasury Stock Method With Compensation Cost
questions.stem: A company has 50,000 stock options outstanding with an exercise price of $20. The average market price during the period is $50, and the average unrecognized compensation cost attributable to future service is $5 per option. Applying the treasury stock method (including unrecognized compensation in assumed proceeds), how many incremental shares are added to the diluted EPS denominator?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 30,000 shares

choices.label: B
choices.text: 50,000 shares

choices.label: C
choices.text: 25,000 shares

choices.label: D
choices.text: 20,000 shares

questions.explanation: Assumed proceeds per option = exercise price $20 + unrecognized compensation $5 = $25, per ASC 260-10-45-29. Total assumed proceeds = 50,000 × $25 = $1,250,000. Shares assumed repurchased at the average market price = $1,250,000 ÷ $50 = 25,000. Incremental shares = 50,000 options assumed issued − 25,000 repurchased = 25,000. Omitting the $5 unrecognized compensation from proceeds gives proceeds of $1,000,000, repurchasing only 20,000 shares and overstating the increment at 30,000. Adding all 50,000 ignores the buyback entirely.
questions.explanationJa: 1オプション当たり想定収入 = 行使価格$20 + 未認識報酬$5 = $25（ASC 260-10-45-29）。想定収入総額 = 50,000 × $25 = $1,250,000。平均市場価格での買戻し想定株式数 = $1,250,000 ÷ $50 = 25,000。増分株式 = 想定発行50,000 − 買戻し25,000 = 25,000。未認識報酬$5を収入から除くと収入は$1,000,000となり買戻しは20,000株のみで増分を30,000と過大計上する。全50,000を加えると買戻しを完全に無視する。

questions.stem: A company grants share-settled restricted stock units classified as equity. The stock price is $40 at grant, $55 at the end of Year 1, and $48 at the end of Year 2 (the vesting date). On what amount is total compensation cost for the award based?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The $48 fair value at the vesting date

choices.label: B
choices.text: The $40 grant-date fair value, with no subsequent remeasurement

choices.label: C
choices.text: The $55 highest fair value reached during the vesting period

choices.label: D
choices.text: The average of the grant-date and vesting-date fair values

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-30-2 requires equity-classified awards, including share-settled RSUs, to be measured at the grant-date fair value, and that amount is not subsequently remeasured for changes in the stock price. Total compensation cost is based on the $40 grant-date fair value regardless of later price movements to $55 or $48. Only liability-classified awards are remeasured each period. Using the vesting-date price, the peak price, or an average would inappropriately reflect post-grant price changes that equity classification excludes.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-2は、株式決済RSUを含む持分分類報酬を付与日公正価値で測定し、その金額を株価変動について事後再測定しないことを要求する。総報酬費用は、その後$55や$48への価格変動にかかわらず付与日公正価値$40に基づく。毎期再測定されるのは負債分類報酬のみである。権利確定日価格、最高値、または平均を用いると、持分分類が排除する付与後の価格変動を不適切に反映してしまう。

questions.subtopic: Modification - Award Not Expected to Vest
questions.stem: A company modifies stock options by lowering the exercise price, increasing their fair value by $6 per option. However, at the modification date, the affected employee is not expected to satisfy the remaining service condition and is therefore not expected to vest in the award. How is the incremental fair value from this modification treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The incremental fair value is recognized immediately at the modification date

choices.label: B
choices.text: The incremental fair value is added to the original grant-date cost and recognized over the remaining service period

choices.label: C
choices.text: The original grant-date cost is reversed and replaced with the modified fair value

choices.label: D
choices.text: No incremental compensation cost is recognized unless and until the employee is expected to vest

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-20-35-3, incremental compensation cost from a modification is recognized only for awards that are expected to vest. If, at the modification date, the award is not expected to vest based on its service or performance conditions, no incremental cost (and no original cost) is recognized at that time. If expectations later change and the award becomes expected to vest, the entity then recognizes the appropriate cost. Recognizing the incremental value immediately or over the service period would improperly book cost for an award the employee is not expected to earn.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-3により、修正による増分報酬費用は確定が見込まれる報酬についてのみ認識する。修正日において勤務条件または業績条件に基づき確定が見込まれない場合、その時点で増分費用（および原費用）は認識しない。後に見込みが変わり確定が見込まれるようになれば、企業はその時点で適切な費用を認識する。増分価値を即時または勤務期間にわたり認識することは、従業員が獲得を見込まれない報酬の費用を不適切に計上することになる。

questions.stem: A company modifies a cash-settled award (liability-classified) so that it will now be settled only in shares (equity-classified). At the modification date, the carrying amount of the liability is $90,000 and the equity-based fair value of the modified award is $110,000. How is this change in classification accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The $90,000 liability is reclassified to equity at the modification date, and the modification is then evaluated for incremental cost

choices.label: B
choices.text: The full $110,000 is recognized immediately as compensation expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: The liability is derecognized and the difference of $20,000 is recorded as a gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: No entry is made because the total fair value is unchanged

questions.explanation: When a modification changes an award's classification from liability to equity, ASC 718-20-35-7 first requires the liability to be measured at its modification-date fair value (the $90,000 carrying amount, already remeasured), and that amount is reclassified into equity at the modification date. The modification is then assessed for any incremental compensation cost like any other modification, recognized over the remaining service period if the award is expected to vest. The change does not trigger immediate full-expense recognition of $110,000, nor a gain; the existing liability balance carries into equity rather than being eliminated with no effect.
questions.explanationJa: 修正により報酬の分類が負債から持分へ変わる場合、ASC 718-20-35-7はまず負債を修正日公正価値（既に再測定済みの繰越額$90,000）で測定し、その金額を修正日に持分へ振り替えることを要求する。次に修正は他の修正と同様に増分報酬費用について評価され、確定が見込まれれば残存勤務期間にわたり認識する。この変更は$110,000の即時全額費用認識も利益の計上も生じさせず、既存の負債残高は消去されるのではなく持分へ繰り越される。

questions.stem: Under ASC 718-50, an employee stock purchase plan is noncompensatory only if it meets all required criteria. Which of the following plan features would still allow the plan to be classified as noncompensatory?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A 12% purchase discount available to all employees

choices.label: B
choices.text: A 6-month look-back feature that sets the purchase price at the lower of the beginning or ending price

choices.label: C
choices.text: A 4% purchase discount, no look-back, available to substantially all full-time employees on an equal basis

choices.label: D
choices.text: Participation limited to senior executives only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-50-25-1, an ESPP is noncompensatory only if: (1) the discount from market price is 5% or less (or the entity can justify a larger discount as not exceeding a per-share cost of issuing shares to the public), (2) substantially all full-time employees meeting limited employment qualifications may participate on an equitable basis, and (3) the plan has no option features such as a look-back. A 4% discount with no look-back available to substantially all employees meets all three criteria. A 12% discount exceeds the 5% threshold, a look-back feature is a disqualifying option feature, and limiting participation to senior executives fails the broad-participation requirement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-50-25-1により、ESPPが非報酬型となるのは次の全てを満たす場合のみである。(1)市場価格からの割引が5%以下（または株式公募コストを超えないと正当化できる場合）、(2)限定的な雇用要件を満たす実質的に全ての常勤従業員が公平に参加できる、(3)ルックバック等のオプション要素がない。実質的に全従業員が利用できるルックバックなしの4%割引は3要件全てを満たす。12%割引は5%基準を超え、ルックバックは不適格となるオプション要素であり、上級役員のみへの限定は広範な参加要件を満たさない。

questions.subtopic: Journal Entry - Credit Account by Classification
questions.stem: When a company records periodic compensation expense for share-based awards, the credit side of the entry depends on the award's classification. Which of the following correctly pairs the classification with the account credited?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Equity-classified options credit a liability; cash-settled SARs credit additional paid-in capital

choices.label: B
choices.text: Equity-classified options credit additional paid-in capital; cash-settled SARs credit a liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both equity-classified options and cash-settled SARs credit a liability

choices.label: D
choices.text: Both equity-classified options and cash-settled SARs credit additional paid-in capital

questions.explanation: For equity-classified awards such as standard stock options, the periodic entry debits compensation expense and credits additional paid-in capital (e.g., APIC — Stock Options), building equity over the service period (ASC 718-10-25-2). For liability-classified awards such as cash-settled SARs, the entry debits compensation expense and credits a liability, which is remeasured to fair value each period (ASC 718-30-25-1). The crediting account is therefore equity for equity-classified awards and a liability for cash-settled awards, so pairing equity options with a liability or cash-settled SARs with paid-in capital is incorrect.
questions.explanationJa: 通常のストックオプション等の持分分類報酬では、定期仕訳は報酬費用を借方、追加払込資本（例：APIC—ストックオプション）を貸方とし、勤務期間にわたり持分を積み上げる（ASC 718-10-25-2）。現金決済SAR等の負債分類報酬では、報酬費用を借方、負債を貸方とし、その負債は毎期公正価値に再測定される（ASC 718-30-25-1）。したがって貸方勘定は持分分類報酬では持分、現金決済報酬では負債となる。持分オプションを負債に、現金決済SARを払込資本に対応させるのは誤りである。

questions.stem: In a business combination, the acquirer is obligated to replace the acquiree's outstanding employee share-based awards. The total grant-date fair value of the replacement awards is $90,000. The acquiree's employees had completed 3 years of a required 4-year vesting period as of the acquisition date, and the replacement awards require no additional postcombination service beyond the original schedule. What amount of the replacement awards' fair value is attributed to postcombination compensation expense?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-30-30-9 through 30-13, the fair value of replacement awards is allocated between precombination service (part of the consideration transferred in the business combination) and postcombination service (compensation expense). The precombination portion = total fair value × (service rendered before the combination ÷ total service period) = $90,000 × (3 ÷ 4) = $67,500, included in purchase consideration. The postcombination portion = $90,000 − $67,500 = $22,500, recognized as compensation expense over the remaining service period. Treating the entire $90,000 as expense ignores the precombination allocation, and $67,500 is the consideration portion rather than the expense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-30-30-9から30-13により、代替報酬の公正価値は結合前勤務（企業結合で移転された対価の一部）と結合後勤務（報酬費用）に配分される。結合前部分 = 公正価値総額 × (結合前に提供された勤務 ÷ 総勤務期間) = $90,000 × (3 ÷ 4) = $67,500で取得対価に含まれる。結合後部分 = $90,000 − $67,500 = $22,500で残存勤務期間にわたり報酬費用として認識する。$90,000全額を費用とするのは結合前配分を無視し、$67,500は費用ではなく対価部分である。

questions.subtopic: RSU - Dividend Equivalents on Forfeited Units
questions.stem: A company grants RSUs that accrue dividend equivalents during the vesting period. The dividend equivalents are paid in cash only on RSUs that ultimately vest; if RSUs are forfeited, any accrued dividend equivalents on those units are also forfeited. How should dividend equivalents paid on RSUs that ultimately vest be accounted for?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a reduction of retained earnings, consistent with dividends on outstanding shares

choices.label: B
choices.text: As additional compensation expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a reduction of additional paid-in capital

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a financing cash outflow with no income statement effect, regardless of vesting

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-55-45, dividend equivalents on equity-classified share-based awards are charged to retained earnings (treated like dividends) when they are paid on awards that ultimately vest. Because the dividend equivalents here are forfeitable and are paid only on units that vest, the amounts paid on vested units are accounted for as a reduction of retained earnings, the same as dividends on outstanding shares. Dividend equivalents accrued on units that are later forfeited would instead be reclassified as additional compensation cost. Therefore, recognizing the payments on vested units as compensation expense or as a reduction of paid-in capital is incorrect.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-55-45により、持分分類報酬の配当相当額は、最終的に確定する報酬について支払われる場合、利益剰余金に賦課する（配当と同様に扱う）。本件の配当相当額は没収可能で確定したユニットについてのみ支払われるため、確定ユニットに支払われる金額は発行済株式への配当と同様に利益剰余金の減少として処理する。後に没収されるユニットについて発生した配当相当額は、代わりに追加報酬費用に振り替えられる。したがって確定ユニットへの支払を報酬費用や払込資本の減少として認識するのは誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Nonpublic Entity - Practical Expedient Expected Term
questions.stem: Under ASU 2021-07, what practical expedient is available to a nonpublic entity when valuing share-based payment awards under ASC 718?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It may measure all awards at intrinsic value with no subsequent remeasurement

choices.label: B
choices.text: It may use the contractual term as the expected term for all option grants

choices.label: C
choices.text: It may use the reasonable application of a reasonable valuation method to determine the current price input (fair value of the underlying share)

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is exempt from recognizing any compensation cost for service-based awards

questions.explanation: ASU 2021-07 added a practical expedient under ASC 718-10-30-20A allowing a nonpublic entity to determine the current price input of its equity shares (used as an input to an option-pricing model) using the reasonable application of a reasonable valuation method. The expedient describes characteristics of an acceptable valuation, aligning with the approach used for certain Internal Revenue Code Section 409A valuations, and reduces the cost and complexity of estimating the underlying share value. It does not permit measuring awards at intrinsic value, using the contractual term as the expected term for all grants, or exempting service-based awards from compensation cost.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2021-07はASC 718-10-30-20Aに実務上の簡便法を追加し、非公開企業がオプション価格モデルのインプットとして用いる持分株式の現在価格インプットを、合理的な評価方法の合理的な適用により決定することを認めた。この簡便法は許容される評価の特徴を示し、内国歳入法第409A条の一部評価で用いられる手法と整合し、原資産株式価値の見積りのコストと複雑性を軽減する。これは報酬を本源的価値で測定すること、全ての付与で契約期間を予想期間とすること、または勤務条件付報酬を報酬費用から免除することは認めていない。

questions.subtopic: Forfeitures As They Occur - Expense Reversal
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Hale Corp. grants 30,000 stock options with a grant-date fair value of $8 per option and a 3-year cliff vesting period. Hale has elected to account for forfeitures as they occur. In Year 2, employees holding 3,000 unvested options terminate employment and forfeit their options. No other forfeitures occur. What amount of compensation expense should Hale recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: Under the as-they-occur policy, previously recognized expense for forfeited unvested awards is reversed in the period of forfeiture. Year 1 expense was 30,000 × $8 × 1/3 = $80,000. After the Year 2 forfeitures, cumulative expense through Year 2 should reflect only the 27,000 remaining options: 27,000 × $8 × 2/3 = $144,000. Year 2 expense is therefore $144,000 − $80,000 = $64,000. The amount of $80,000 ignores the forfeitures entirely; $72,000 results from spreading the remaining options' total cost over 3 years without the cumulative catch-down (27,000 × $8 ÷ 3); $144,000 is the cumulative total through Year 2, not the Year 2 expense alone.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-3およびASU 2016-09により、失効（forfeiture）を発生時に認識する方針を選択した場合、未確定アワードが失効した期に過年度計上額を戻し入れます。Year 1の費用は 30,000個 × $8 × 1/3 = $80,000。Year 2末時点の累計費用は残存27,000個ベースで 27,000 × $8 × 2/3 = $144,000 となるべきなので、Year 2の費用は $144,000 − $80,000 = $64,000 です。$80,000は失効を無視した金額、$72,000は累計調整を行わず残存分を単純に3年で割った金額（27,000 × $8 ÷ 3）、$144,000はYear 2単年ではなく累計額です。

questions.subtopic: Estimated Forfeiture Rate - Cumulative Catch-Up on Revision
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Marlow Inc. grants 50,000 stock options with a grant-date fair value of $6 per option and a 4-year cliff vesting period. Marlow estimates forfeitures and initially expects 20% of the options to be forfeited. At the end of Year 3, Marlow revises its estimate and now expects only 10% to be forfeited. What amount of compensation expense should Marlow recognize in Year 3?
questions.explanation: A change in the estimated forfeiture rate is accounted for with a cumulative catch-up adjustment in the period of change. Total grant-date fair value is 50,000 × $6 = $300,000. Cumulative expense through Year 2 was based on 80% vesting: $300,000 × 80% × 2/4 = $120,000. Cumulative expense required through Year 3 at the revised 90% estimate is $300,000 × 90% × 3/4 = $202,500. Year 3 expense is $202,500 − $120,000 = $82,500. The amount of $67,500 is one year's expense at the new rate without the catch-up ($300,000 × 90% ÷ 4); $60,000 is one year's expense at the old rate; $202,500 is the cumulative total, not the Year 3 charge.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-3に基づき、失効見積率の変更は変更期に累積キャッチアップ調整として処理します。付与日公正価値総額は 50,000 × $6 = $300,000。Year 2末までの累計費用は確定率80%前提で $300,000 × 80% × 2/4 = $120,000。Year 3末に必要な累計費用は改訂後の確定率90%で $300,000 × 90% × 3/4 = $202,500。よってYear 3の費用は $202,500 − $120,000 = $82,500 です。$67,500は累積調整を行わない新レートでの単年費用、$60,000は旧レートでの単年費用、$202,500は累計額であり単年費用ではありません。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Quill Co. grants 6,000 stock options that vest in three equal annual tranches of 2,000 options each on December 31 of Years 1, 2, and 3. Quill values each tranche separately: the grant-date fair values are $9 per option for the first tranche, $12 for the second, and $15 for the third. Quill uses the accelerated (graded-vesting / tranche-by-tranche) attribution method. What amount of compensation expense should Quill recognize in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the accelerated method, each tranche is treated as a separate award and expensed over its own vesting period. Year 1 expense: Tranche 1 (vests in 1 year): 2,000 × $9 = $18,000; Tranche 2 (vests over 2 years): 2,000 × $12 × 1/2 = $12,000; Tranche 3 (vests over 3 years): 2,000 × $15 × 1/3 = $10,000. Total Year 1 expense is $18,000 + $12,000 + $10,000 = $40,000. The amount of $24,000 is the straight-line result (total cost of $72,000 ÷ 3); $72,000 is the total compensation cost for the entire award; $30,000 results from incorrectly averaging the tranche fair values.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-8に基づき、加速法（tranche-by-tranche法）では各トランシェを独立のアワードとみなし、それぞれの権利確定期間で費用化します。Year 1の費用：第1トランシェ（1年で確定）2,000 × $9 = $18,000、第2トランシェ（2年）2,000 × $12 × 1/2 = $12,000、第3トランシェ（3年）2,000 × $15 × 1/3 = $10,000。合計 $40,000 です。$24,000は定額法による金額（総額$72,000 ÷ 3年）、$72,000はアワード全体の総費用、$30,000はトランシェ公正価値を誤って平均した結果です。

questions.stem: A company grants stock options with graded vesting (service condition only) and elects the straight-line attribution method permitted by ASC 718. Which of the following constraints applies to the straight-line election?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cumulative compensation expense may never exceed the total grant-date fair value of the vested tranches.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The straight-line method may be used only if all tranches have the same grant-date fair value per option.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The election must be made award by award and may differ for each grant.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cumulative compensation expense recognized at any date must be at least equal to the grant-date fair value of the portion of the award that has vested as of that date.

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-35-8 permits straight-line attribution for awards with graded vesting and only a service condition, but imposes a floor: cumulative compensation cost recognized at any date must at least equal the grant-date fair value of the vested portion of the award at that date. The statement that cumulative expense may never exceed the vested tranches' fair value reverses the constraint — it is a minimum, not a maximum. There is no requirement that all tranches share the same per-option fair value. The attribution method is an accounting policy applied consistently to similar awards, not an award-by-award election.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-8は、勤務条件のみの段階的権利確定アワードについて定額法を認めていますが、「任意の時点における累計費用は、その時点で権利確定済み部分の付与日公正価値を下回ってはならない」というフロア（下限）制約を課しています。確定済み部分の公正価値を上限とする記述は制約の向きが逆です（下限であって上限ではない）。全トランシェの単価が同一である必要はありません。また帰属方法は類似のアワードに首尾一貫して適用すべき会計方針であり、アワードごとに選択するものではありません。

questions.subtopic: Market Condition Achieved but Service Condition Failed
questions.stem: Verity Corp. grants stock options that vest only if (1) the employee provides 3 years of service and (2) the stock price reaches $90 (a market condition). The grant-date fair value, which incorporates the market condition, is $120,000. The stock price reaches $90 in Year 1, but the employee voluntarily terminates employment at the end of Year 2, before completing the required service. How should Verity account for the compensation cost previously recognized?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reverse the previously recognized compensation expense, because the requisite service was not rendered.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Retain the previously recognized expense, because the market condition was achieved before termination.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Retain the previously recognized expense and accelerate the remaining unrecognized cost.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reclassify the previously recognized expense from compensation expense to a loss on forfeiture.

questions.explanation: The treatment of market conditions cuts only one way: compensation cost is not reversed when a market condition fails, provided the requisite service is rendered. When the service condition itself is not satisfied — the employee leaves before completing the 3-year requisite service period — the award is forfeited and all previously recognized compensation cost is reversed, regardless of whether the market condition was achieved. Retaining or accelerating the expense would be appropriate only if service had been completed. There is no reclassification to a forfeiture loss under ASC 718.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-2および35-14の市場条件の取扱いは一方向のみです。すなわち、勤務条件を満たしたうえで市場条件のみ未達成の場合は費用を戻し入れませんが、本問のように勤務条件自体が未達成（3年の必要勤務期間前に退職）の場合、アワードは失効し、市場条件の達成有無にかかわらず過年度計上費用をすべて戻し入れます。費用の維持や残額の加速認識は勤務完了時のみ妥当であり、「失効損失」への振替という処理はASC 718に存在しません。

questions.subtopic: Cash-Settled SAR - Year 2 Expense After Fair Value Decline
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Brandt Co. grants 10,000 cash-settled stock appreciation rights (SARs) to employees with a 2-year cliff vesting period. The fair value per SAR is $4 at the grant date, $7 at December 31, Year 1, and $6 at December 31, Year 2. What amount of compensation expense should Brandt recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: Cash-settled SARs are liability-classified awards remeasured at fair value each reporting date, with expense recognized based on the percentage of the service period elapsed. The liability at December 31, Year 1 is 10,000 × $7 × 1/2 = $35,000 (which equals Year 1 expense). At December 31, Year 2 the awards are fully vested, so the liability is 10,000 × $6 = $60,000. Year 2 expense is the increase in the liability: $60,000 − $35,000 = $25,000. The amount of $35,000 is the Year 1 expense; $30,000 results from simply halving the ending liability; $60,000 is the total liability balance, not the Year 2 expense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-30-35-3により、現金決済型SARは負債分類として各報告日に公正価値で再測定し、勤務期間の経過割合に応じて費用を認識します。Year 1末の負債は 10,000 × $7 × 1/2 = $35,000（＝Year 1費用）。Year 2末は全数権利確定済みのため負債は 10,000 × $6 = $60,000。Year 2の費用は負債の増加額 $60,000 − $35,000 = $25,000 です。$35,000はYear 1の費用、$30,000は期末負債を単純に半分にした誤り、$60,000は期末負債残高でありYear 2単年の費用ではありません。

questions.stem: Doran Inc. has 8,000 fully vested cash-settled stock appreciation rights outstanding. The related liability has a carrying amount of $50,000. All 8,000 SARs are exercised on the same date, when the intrinsic value is $7 per SAR, and Doran pays the holders in cash. What additional compensation expense, if any, should Doran recognize at settlement?
questions.explanation: A liability-classified award is remeasured to fair value through the settlement date, so the total compensation cost ultimately equals the cash paid. The settlement amount is 8,000 × $7 = $56,000. Because the liability is carried at $50,000, an additional $6,000 of compensation expense is recognized at settlement (debit compensation expense $6,000, debit SAR liability $50,000, credit cash $56,000). An answer of $0 would be correct only if the carrying amount already equaled the settlement value; $56,000 is the total cash paid, not the incremental expense; $50,000 is the pre-settlement liability balance.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-30-35-3により、負債分類のアワードは決済日まで公正価値で再測定されるため、最終的な報酬費用合計は支払現金額と一致します。決済額は 8,000 × $7 = $56,000。負債計上額が $50,000 のため、決済時に追加費用 $6,000 を認識します（借方：報酬費用 6,000／SAR負債 50,000、貸方：現金 56,000）。追加費用 $0 は帳簿価額が決済額と一致している場合のみ正しく、$56,000 は支払総額、$50,000 は決済前の負債残高です。

questions.stem: Pemberton Co. sponsors an employee stock purchase plan that allows employees to buy shares at an 8% discount from the market price on the purchase date. The plan has no look-back or other option features, and Pemberton cannot justify the discount as comparable to offers made to other holders or as equal to avoided issuance costs. An employee purchases 1,000 shares when the market price is $50, paying $46 per share. What amount of compensation expense should Pemberton recognize for this purchase?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-50, a plan is noncompensatory only if the discount does not exceed 5% (or a greater discount can be justified), substantially all eligible employees may participate, and the plan has no option features. An 8% unjustified discount makes the plan compensatory, and the entire discount — not merely the portion above 5% — is compensation: 1,000 shares × ($50 − $46) = $4,000. An expense of $0 would apply only to a noncompensatory plan; $1,500 incorrectly charges only the discount in excess of 5% (1,000 × $1.50); $2,500 incorrectly charges a 5% discount (1,000 × $2.50).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-50-25-1により、ESPPが非報酬型（noncompensatory）となるのは、割引率が5%以下（またはそれを超える割引の正当化が可能）、実質的に全従業員が参加可能、オプション的特徴がない、という要件をすべて満たす場合のみです。正当化できない8%割引のプランは報酬型であり、5%超過部分だけでなく割引額全額が報酬費用となります：1,000株 × ($50 − $46) = $4,000。$0は非報酬型の場合のみ、$1,500は5%超過分のみ（1,000 × $1.50）を費用化した誤り、$2,500は5%分（1,000 × $2.50）を費用化した誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Modification of Unvested Options - Subsequent Annual Expense
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Sutter Corp. grants 2,000 stock options with a grant-date fair value of $6 per option and a 3-year cliff vesting period. On December 31, Year 1, after recognizing Year 1 expense, Sutter reduces the exercise price. Immediately before the modification, the fair value of each option is $2; immediately after, it is $5. The vesting period is unchanged and the options are expected to vest. What amount of compensation expense should Sutter recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: A modification adds incremental fair value — the excess of the modified award's fair value over the original award's fair value immediately before modification — to the unrecognized original cost. Original total cost: 2,000 × $6 = $12,000; Year 1 expense: $4,000; unrecognized original cost: $8,000. Incremental fair value: 2,000 × ($5 − $2) = $6,000. Total remaining cost of $8,000 + $6,000 = $14,000 is recognized over the remaining 2 years, or $7,000 per year. The amount of $4,000 ignores the incremental value; $10,000 incorrectly spreads only the incremental value plus one year of original cost; $14,000 is the total remaining cost, not the Year 2 expense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-3により、条件変更時は「変更後公正価値－変更直前公正価値」の増分公正価値を、未認識の当初費用に加算して残存期間で認識します。当初総費用：2,000 × $6 = $12,000、Year 1費用：$4,000、未認識残：$8,000。増分公正価値：2,000 × ($5 − $2) = $6,000。残存認識額は $8,000 + $6,000 = $14,000 で、残り2年にわたり年 $7,000 ずつ認識します。$4,000は増分価値を無視した金額、$10,000は計算要素の組合せ誤り、$14,000は残存総額でありYear 2単年の費用ではありません。

questions.stem: Wexford Co. cancels unvested stock options held by employees and does not grant any replacement award or other consideration concurrently with the cancellation. At the cancellation date, $90,000 of grant-date compensation cost remains unrecognized. How should Wexford account for the cancellation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reverse all compensation cost previously recognized for the cancelled options.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize the remaining $90,000 of unrecognized compensation cost immediately at the cancellation date.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Continue to recognize the remaining $90,000 over the original vesting period.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize no further compensation cost and leave previously recognized amounts unchanged.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-20-35-9, a cancellation that is not accompanied by a concurrent grant of a replacement award is treated as an acceleration of vesting: all remaining unrecognized compensation cost is recognized immediately at the cancellation date. Reversing previously recognized cost is the treatment for a forfeiture due to failure to satisfy a service condition, not for an employer-initiated cancellation. Continuing recognition over the original vesting period ignores that the award no longer exists. Stopping recognition without expensing the remaining cost would understate total compensation for services the entity contracted for.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-9により、代替アワードの同時付与を伴わないキャンセル（cancellation）は権利確定の加速として扱い、未認識の残存費用 $90,000 をキャンセル日に即時認識します。過年度計上額の戻入れは勤務条件未達成による失効の処理であり、会社都合のキャンセルには適用されません。当初の権利確定期間にわたる認識の継続はアワードが消滅している事実と矛盾し、残存費用を認識せず打ち切る処理は報酬費用の過少計上となります。

questions.subtopic: Restricted Stock - Year 3 Expense With Actual Forfeitures
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Calder Inc. grants 9,000 shares of restricted stock to employees when the stock price is $18 per share. The shares cliff-vest after 3 years of service. Calder accounts for forfeitures as they occur. In Year 3, employees holding 600 unvested shares terminate employment and forfeit their shares; the remaining 8,400 shares vest on December 31, Year 3. What amount of compensation expense should Calder recognize in Year 3?
questions.explanation: Restricted stock is measured at the grant-date stock price. Expense in Years 1 and 2 was 9,000 × $18 × 1/3 = $54,000 per year, or $108,000 cumulatively. After the Year 3 forfeitures, cumulative expense must equal the cost of the shares that actually vest: 8,400 × $18 = $151,200. Year 3 expense is $151,200 − $108,000 = $43,200, which combines the normal third-year charge with the reversal of cost previously recognized on the 600 forfeited shares. The amount of $54,000 ignores the forfeitures; $50,400 spreads the vested shares' cost over 3 years without the cumulative adjustment (8,400 × $18 ÷ 3); $151,200 is the total cost of the award, not the Year 3 expense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30およびASC 718-10-35-3により、制限株式は付与日の株価で測定します。Year 1・2の費用は各年 9,000 × $18 × 1/3 = $54,000、累計 $108,000。Year 3に600株が失効したため、累計費用は実際に確定した株式のコスト 8,400 × $18 = $151,200 と一致させる必要があります。Year 3の費用は $151,200 − $108,000 = $43,200 で、3年目の通常費用と失効株600株分の戻入れの純額です。$54,000は失効を無視、$50,400は累積調整なしで確定株コストを単純に3等分（8,400 × $18 ÷ 3）、$151,200はアワード総費用です。

questions.subtopic: Service Inception Date Precedes Grant Date
questions.stem: On October 1, Year 1, Norwich Co. and a new executive agree that the executive will receive a stock option award, and the executive begins providing the requisite service immediately. However, the award is not authorized by the board of directors until February 1, Year 2, which is when the grant date occurs. How should Norwich account for compensation cost between October 1, Year 1 and February 1, Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize no compensation cost until the grant date, then recognize cost prospectively from February 1, Year 2.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize compensation cost beginning October 1, Year 1, measured at the intrinsic value of the award on that date and never remeasured.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Defer all compensation cost until the award vests, because no grant date exists during the service period.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize compensation cost beginning October 1, Year 1, based on the award's fair value remeasured at each reporting date until the grant date is established.

questions.explanation: When the service inception date precedes the grant date, ASC 718 requires compensation cost to be accrued from the service inception date based on the fair value of the award estimated at each reporting date, with remeasurement continuing until the grant date is established; at the grant date, the measurement is fixed at grant-date fair value. Delaying recognition until the grant date would omit cost for service already rendered. Intrinsic value at the service inception date is not the required measure. Deferring all cost until vesting has no basis in ASC 718.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-55-108により、勤務開始日（service inception date）が付与日（grant date）に先行する場合、勤務開始日から費用の計上を開始し、付与日が確定するまで各報告日に公正価値を再見積りします。付与日成立時点で測定額は付与日公正価値に固定されます。付与日まで認識を開始しない処理は既提供の勤務に対する費用の脱漏となり、勤務開始日の本源的価値による固定測定も要求される方法ではありません。権利確定まで全費用を繰り延べる処理にASC 718上の根拠はありません。

questions.subtopic: Performance Condition Never Achieved - Lifetime Expense
questions.stem: Ashby Corp. grants stock options with a total grant-date fair value of $120,000 that vest only upon achievement of a specified cumulative earnings target (a performance condition) over a 3-year service period. The target was assessed as probable in Years 1 and 2, and Ashby recognized $40,000 of cumulative compensation expense through the end of Year 2. In Year 3, achievement becomes improbable, and the target is ultimately not achieved; the options never vest, although the employees remain employed. What is the total compensation expense recognized over the life of the award?
questions.explanation: Compensation cost for an award with a performance condition is recognized only if the condition is satisfied. When achievement becomes improbable, previously recognized cost is reversed in that period, and because the target is never met, no cost is ever recognized for the award — total lifetime expense is $0. In Year 3, Ashby reverses the $40,000 recognized in Years 1 and 2. A total of $120,000 would apply only if the condition were satisfied; $40,000 ignores the required reversal; $80,000 has no basis. This treatment contrasts with market conditions, for which cost is not reversed when the condition fails.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-2およびASC 718-10-25-20により、業績条件付アワードの費用は条件達成が見込まれる（probable）場合にのみ認識し、達成不能となった期に過年度計上額を戻し入れます。最終的に目標未達成でアワードが確定しなかったため、存続期間全体の費用合計は $0 です（Year 3にYear 1・2計上分 $40,000 を戻入れ）。$120,000は条件達成時のみ、$40,000は戻入れを無視した金額です。なお市場条件の場合は未達成でも費用を戻し入れない点が対照的です。

questions.stem: Granger Manufacturing grants stock options to employees who work directly in the production of inventory. How should Granger account for the share-based compensation cost attributable to these production employees?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expense the cost immediately, because ASC 718 requires all share-based compensation to be recognized in the income statement as incurred.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Treat the cost the same as cash compensation paid to those employees, capitalizing it into inventory and expensing it through cost of goods sold when the inventory is sold.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize the cost directly in equity, because the credit and the debit both relate to equity instruments.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer the cost as a prepaid asset until the options are exercised.

questions.explanation: ASC 718-10-25-2 requires share-based compensation cost to be recognized in the same manner as if the entity had paid cash compensation. Cost attributable to employees whose labor is capitalizable — such as production workers or employees constructing fixed assets — is capitalized into inventory (or the asset) and flows to expense through cost of goods sold or depreciation. Immediate expensing is required only when the related cash compensation would also be expensed. Recognizing the cost directly in equity would bypass the income statement entirely, and deferral as a prepaid asset until exercise has no basis in ASC 718.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-2により、株式報酬費用は現金給与を支払った場合と同様に処理します。製造要員や固定資産の自家建設に従事する従業員など、労務費が資産化対象となる従業員に係る株式報酬は棚卸資産（または当該資産）に資産化し、売上原価や減価償却を通じて費用化します。即時費用化が求められるのは、対応する現金給与も費用処理される場合のみです。資本に直接認識する処理は損益計算書を完全に迂回することになり、行使時まで前払資産として繰り延べる処理に根拠はありません。

questions.subtopic: Option Exercise - APIC in Excess of Par
questions.stem: Employees of Halpern Co. exercise 2,000 fully vested stock options with an exercise price of $15 per share. The common stock has a par value of $1 per share, and the balance in APIC — stock options related to these options is $8,000. What amount should Halpern credit to additional paid-in capital in excess of par upon exercise?
questions.explanation: Upon exercise, the entity records cash received of 2,000 × $15 = $30,000, eliminates the $8,000 APIC — stock options balance, credits common stock for par of 2,000 × $1 = $2,000, and credits the remainder to APIC in excess of par: $30,000 + $8,000 − $2,000 = $36,000. The amount of $28,000 omits the reclassified option APIC and the cash combination ($30,000 − $2,000); $30,000 is just the cash proceeds; $38,000 fails to deduct the par value credited to common stock.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-20-35-2の枠組みでの行使時仕訳は、（借方）現金 2,000 × $15 = $30,000、（借方）APIC—ストックオプション $8,000、（貸方）資本金（額面）2,000 × $1 = $2,000、（貸方）APIC—額面超過額 $36,000（= 30,000 + 8,000 − 2,000）となります。$28,000はオプション分APICの振替を漏らした金額（30,000 − 2,000）、$30,000は現金受取額のみ、$38,000は額面相当の資本金計上 $2,000 を控除し忘れた金額です。

questions.stem: Renwick Co. grants shares to employees that vest after 2 years. Each employee holds a put right allowing the employee to require Renwick to repurchase the vested shares for cash at fair value beginning three months after vesting. How should Renwick classify the award?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Equity, because the repurchase price is fair value rather than a fixed amount.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Equity, because classification is based solely on the legal form of the instrument as shares.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Liability only if the employees are members of management.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Liability, because the put right allows the employee to avoid bearing the risks and rewards of share ownership for a reasonable period (at least six months) after vesting.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 718-10-25-9, an award of shares with a repurchase feature is classified as a liability if the employee can put the shares back to the employer before bearing the risks and rewards of ownership for a reasonable period — defined as at least six months — after the shares vest. A put exercisable three months after vesting fails this test, so the award is a liability even though the repurchase price is fair value. A fair value repurchase price does not by itself preserve equity classification when the holding-period test fails, legal form is not the classification criterion, and the holder's management status is irrelevant to the test.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-9により、従業員が権利確定後「合理的期間（最低6か月）」株式所有のリスクと経済価値を負担する前に会社へ売り戻せるプット権付き株式は負債に分類されます。本問のプットは確定後3か月で行使可能なため6か月基準を満たさず、買戻価格が公正価値であっても負債分類となります。買戻価格が公正価値であること自体は保有期間テスト不合格を治癒せず、法形式（株式であること）は分類基準ではなく、保有者が経営者か否かも判定に無関係です。

questions.subtopic: Unrecognized Compensation Cost - End of Year 1
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Tilden Corp. grants 18,000 stock options with a grant-date fair value of $7 per option and a 3-year cliff vesting period. All options are expected to vest, and no forfeitures occur. What is the amount of unrecognized compensation cost remaining at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Total compensation cost is 18,000 × $7 = $126,000, recognized straight-line over the 3-year requisite service period. Year 1 expense is $126,000 × 1/3 = $42,000, leaving unrecognized cost of $126,000 − $42,000 = $84,000 at December 31, Year 1. The amount of $42,000 is the Year 1 expense rather than the remaining cost; $126,000 is the total cost before any recognition; $63,000 incorrectly assumes half of the cost has been recognized after one year of a 3-year period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-35-2により、報酬費用総額は 18,000 × $7 = $126,000 で、3年の必要勤務期間にわたり定額で認識します。Year 1の費用は $126,000 × 1/3 = $42,000 なので、Year 1末の未認識報酬費用は $126,000 − $42,000 = $84,000 です。$42,000はYear 1の費用そのもの、$126,000は認識前の総額、$63,000は3年契約の1年経過時点で半分認識済みと誤って仮定した金額です。なお未認識費用とその加重平均認識期間はASC 718の開示項目でもあります。

questions.subtopic: Conditions Excluded from Grant-Date Fair Value
questions.stem: Under ASC 718, how do service conditions and performance conditions (other than market conditions) affect the measurement of an equity-classified employee stock option award?

choices.label: A
choices.text: They reduce the grant-date fair value per option through an adjustment to the option-pricing model.

choices.label: B
choices.text: They are excluded from the grant-date fair value per option and instead affect the number of awards for which compensation cost is recognized.

choices.label: C
choices.text: They require remeasurement of the award's fair value at each reporting date until the condition is resolved.

choices.label: D
choices.text: They are reflected by discounting the grant-date fair value for the probability of achievement.

questions.explanation: Service and performance conditions are vesting conditions that are not reflected in the grant-date fair value of the option itself. Instead, they determine the quantity of awards for which cost is recognized: expense is accrued for the number of awards expected to (and that ultimately do) vest, with changes in expectations handled through cumulative catch-up adjustments. Only market conditions are incorporated into the fair value measure through the valuation model. Equity-classified awards are not remeasured after the grant date, and probability-weighting the per-option fair value is not the prescribed mechanism for service or performance conditions.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-30-27により、勤務条件と業績条件（市場条件以外）はオプション自体の付与日公正価値には反映せず、費用認識の対象となるアワードの「数量」に反映します。すなわち確定見込数量に基づいて費用を計上し、見込みの変更は累積キャッチアップで調整します。評価モデルを通じて公正価値に織り込むのは市場条件のみです。持分分類のアワードは付与日後に再測定せず、達成確率で単価を割り引く方法も勤務・業績条件について規定された処理ではありません。

questions.subtopic: Business Combination - Replacement Award Allocation Calculation
questions.stem: In acquiring Target Co., Acquirer Inc. is obligated to issue replacement stock options to Target's employees. The fair value of the replacement awards equals the fair value of the original Target awards: $100,000. At the acquisition date, the employees have completed 3 years of the original 4-year requisite service period, and the replacement awards require 1 additional year of post-combination service. What amount of the replacement awards' fair value is included in the consideration transferred for the business combination?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 805, the portion of a replacement award attributable to precombination service is included in consideration transferred, computed as the fair value of the acquiree award multiplied by the ratio of the precombination service period to the greater of the total service period or the original service period. Here, $100,000 × 3/4 = $75,000 is part of consideration transferred, and the remaining $25,000 is recognized as post-combination compensation expense over the 1-year remaining service period. The amount of $100,000 ignores the required attribution; $25,000 is the post-combination portion; $50,000 has no basis in the 3-of-4-years service pattern.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-30-30-9により、代替アワードのうち取得前勤務（precombination service）に帰属する部分を企業結合の移転対価に含めます。配分は「被取得企業アワードの公正価値 × 取得前勤務期間 ÷（総勤務期間と当初勤務期間のいずれか大きい方）」で計算します。本問では $100,000 × 3/4 = $75,000 が移転対価、残額 $25,000 が残存1年の勤務期間にわたる取得後の報酬費用となります。$100,000は配分計算を無視、$25,000は取得後費用部分、$50,000は本問の勤務実績（4年中3年完了）から導けない金額です。

questions.subtopic: Income Statement Classification of Stock Compensation
questions.stem: How should share-based compensation expense be classified in the income statement under U.S. GAAP?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Always as a separate line item labeled "share-based compensation expense."

choices.label: B
choices.text: Always within general and administrative expenses, regardless of the employees' functions.

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a component of other comprehensive income until the awards vest.

choices.label: D
choices.text: In the same income statement line items in which the cash compensation of the related employees is classified (e.g., cost of goods sold, selling expense, research and development).

questions.explanation: ASC 718 requires share-based compensation cost to be classified consistently with other compensation of the employees who received the awards — production employees' awards flow through cost of goods sold, sales staff's through selling expense, and so on. There is no requirement to present a single separate income statement line, and forcing all such cost into general and administrative expenses would misstate functional expense classifications. Share-based compensation expense is a component of net income, not other comprehensive income; the corresponding credit (for equity-classified awards) goes to paid-in capital, but the expense itself never bypasses earnings.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 718-10-25-2の原則により、株式報酬費用は当該従業員の現金給与と同じ損益計算書の表示科目に分類します（製造要員は売上原価、販売員は販売費、研究員は研究開発費など）。独立の単一表示科目とする要求はなく、全額を一般管理費に計上すると機能別分類を歪めます。株式報酬費用は純利益の構成要素であり、その他の包括利益（OCI）を経由しません。持分分類アワードの貸方は払込資本ですが、費用そのものは必ず損益を通ります。
